entries.title: Arcos The Wanderer Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Another new one, eh? I can smell it on you.

Veterans tend to smell just a little more like despair.

I'm Arcos, once a sailor, now a wanderer.

You didn't think you were the only one, did you? Hah!

You should go north, there's whole cities of Exiles up there.

entries.title: Arcos The Wanderer Greeting
entries.text:
Back again, Exile? I like familiar faces. Helps me to remember that not everyone dies out here

Seen enough of the desert? Better get used it, it's all we have.

entries.title: Arcos The Wanderer Farewell
entries.text:
Good luck, Exile. It's a tough world out there.

Farewell. I won't pin my hopes on seeing you again.

entries.title: Arcos The Wanderer Conversation 1
entries.text:
In the beginning there were only a few of us.

But now... seems like fresh faced fools are showing up every week.

Not all of them are as friendly as I am, mind you.

Watch your step - there are a lot of desperate people out there.

entries.title: Arcos The Wanderer Conversation 2
entries.text:
The bracelets? You can't remove them.

Saw one fool cut his arm clean off trying to get rid of it.

He bled to death right there on the sand. Nasty business.

Leave it on. You'll start to hear the voices soon enough.

Then you'll start seeing the ghosts.

I don't think the voices are for us.

Not for us here and now. I think they were talking to somebody else.

Back then. Wherever it was.

Whenever it was.

entries.title: Arcos The Wanderer Conversation 3
entries.text:
Those pillars keep us confined here.

Some call it the cursewall, or the ghostfence or just, the boundary.

Doesn't really matter what it's called, does it?

I walked all the way around it once. Had to be careful not to cross it, of course.

Once, in the north, I saw a few people on the other side. Wearing bracelets just like ours but with gems of amber.

It makes you wonder...

entries.title: Arcos The Wanderer Conversation 4
entries.text:
I'm no scholar, but I've met a few who passed through.

They all have mad theories about what the ruins scattered around here are.

Lemurians, Giant-kings, Khari, Valusian Serpent men - do those names mean anything to you?

Never mind. It's my experience that men who have their minds in the past get eaten by crocodiles in the present.

entries.title: Barkeeper F Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Drink or talk? ...or both?

Drink or talk? ...or both?

What can I get you?

What can I get you?

What is your pleasure?

What is your pleasure?

The tavern is the best way to end the day. Or start it. Or while away the middle hours of it.

entries.title: Barkeeper F Greeting
entries.text:
Drink or talk? ...or both?

Drink or talk? ...or both?

What can I get you?

What can I get you?

What is your pleasure?

What is your pleasure?

The tavern is the best way to end the day. Or start it. Or while away the middle hours of it.

entries.title: Barkeeper F Farewell
entries.text:
A favorable day to you.

Go about your business. I'll be here, pouring and listening.

Go about your business. I'll be here, pouring and listening.

Come back soon. I am always open.

Come back soon. I'm always open.

Short days. Long nights.

Short days. Long nights.

entries.title: Barkeeper F Conversation 1
entries.text: Building a tavern was wise, and freeing me to run the bar was wiser still. You shall see!

entries.title: Barkeeper F Conversation 2
entries.text: I once owned a watering hole in Arenjun, full of the most vile, despicable scum of Zamora...not like these fine folk.

entries.title: Barkeeper F Conversation 3
entries.text: Things went sideways. I got caught giving room and board to thieves... the short of it is I became an exile.

entries.title: Barkeeper F Conversation 4
entries.text: But I still have the knack of it. A tongue to convince patrons to order just one more drink... and an ear to lap up all of their spilled secrets.

entries.title: Barkeeper F Conversation 5
entries.text: You build the tavern. You adorn it as you see fit, fill it with seats, and I will take care of the rest. Wish to bribe a contact to visit the tavern? Give me the order and the coin, and I'll send it right along.

entries.title: Barkeeper F Conversation 6
entries.text: I have eyes and ears in the underworld. I know the right people. I know how to get to know the right people I do not yet know. You and I can do very right by one another. You shall see!

What can I get you?

Ahh, what can I get you?

What is your pleasure?

What is your pleasure?

The tavern is the best way to end the day. Or start it. Or while away the middle hours of it.

entries.title: Barkeeper Follower F Initial Greeting
entries.text: I owe you much, but I am wasted as a fighter.

entries.title: Barkeeper Follower F Greeting
entries.text: I owe you much, but I am wasted as a fighter.

entries.title: Barkeeper Follower F Conversation 1
entries.text: My talents are in the barkeep trade. I can keep you and yours happy with drink, and my ears suss out all manner of secrets and gossip.

entries.title: Barkeeper Follower F Conversation 2
entries.text: I will fight for you, but I could do so much more.

entries.title: Barkeeper Follower M Initial Greeting
entries.text: I owe you much, but I am wasted as a fighter.

entries.title: Barkeeper Follower M Conversation 2

entries.title: Blacksmith Ghost Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Walker-amongst-the-dead - what seek you?!

There is nothing here to take. I was robbed of my dearest possessions long before you came.

I’ve only wisdom to offer; I am Thorgar of the North, Blacksmith of the bear clan.

Return what was taken from me and the legacy of Aesir’s greatest smith shall be yours.

entries.title: Blacksmith Ghost Greeting
entries.text: Tread lightly, warrior. It is the bones of my fellow clansmen your boots are trampling on.

entries.title: Blacksmith Ghost Farewell
entries.text: The Mounds of the Dead is no place for the living. Let the dead rest.

entries.title: Blacksmith Ghost Conversation 1
entries.text:
All my life I spent learning of steel, studying it.

Smiths these days see nothing but cold metal where I saw life and diversity, it’s temper and heart.

All that insight I put into runes that my wife guarded like a mother guards a child.

entries.title: Blacksmith Ghost Conversation 2
entries.text:
Godrin - my son… after my departure it fell to him to continue my legacy.

I can still hear his battle-cries at night coming from the south.

Clutching to the tablet, he fights off the thieves who come to claim it.

entries.title: Blacksmith Ghost Conversation 3
entries.text:
I recall that day like it was yesterday; when King Dunkar made me a head blacksmith and gifted a crest to my family.

People whispered that Ymir himself guided my hammer… Those were glorious days.

entries.title: Bounty Collector Conversation 1
entries.text:
Like you, we are strangers in this accursed realm.

We seek a way back to our lands in the misty east.

And though we have crossed the length and breadth of this hellish plane, a way out remains elusive.

But perhaps things have changed.

Perhaps this 'new' sorcery may show us a path to deliverance -- if it can be understood.

To that end, we serve Mek-kamoses, Lord of the Black Ring of Stygia and a fellow prisoner in this forsaken hell.

While the workings of this new sorcery are unknown, unproven, and dangerous, who better to tame its secrets than a master sorcerer?

entries.title: Bounty Collector Conversation 2
entries.text:
If you too wish freedom from this bedeviled land, we urge you to hasten our lord's study of this new magic.

But time is short, for there are others who oppose us.

Rogue sorcerers who seek this same knowledge for their own selfish gain.

We bid you seek out these defilers where they hide.

Seize them and bring them back to our camp – we will take care of the rest.

We will, of course, reward you for your successful efforts.

entries.title: Bounty Collector Conversation 3
entries.text:
There are three ways you may abet our lord's research.

First, you may place the skulls of conquered sorcerers upon the altar for a small reward.

But if it's greater fortune you seek, know that Mek-kamoses has a particular interest in live specimens.

If you have the steel and wit to capture a living sorcerer, return them here and lock them within yon cage for a greater reward.

entries.title: Bounty Collector Conversation 4
entries.text:
Finally, there is one more reward, but it is saved for a rare few who can achieve the impossible.

Above all, our lord seeks the hidden masters who stand behind these rogue sorcerers.

Find them and place their skulls upon the altar.

But beware, you hunt powerful magic wielders – do not attempt to take them alive, their only value to us is as skulls adorning our altar.

Achieve this and the greatest reward will be yours.

entries.title: Bounty Collector Conversation 5
entries.text:
We pay in ancient obolus coins – an ancient currency from a forgotten time.

You may exchange these with our trader for unique goods and chattels.

We have established camps throughout the lands.

Different camps host different traders, and you won't find the same offerings in every place.

Some will offer various armor pieces, weapons, and even thralls -- if they are what you seek.

entries.title: Bounty Slaver Conversation 1
entries.text:
These captives have been found lacking in sorcerous skill and have been set aside by our lord.

Still, their backs are strong and may prove to have a value yet -- as a thrall.

I will trade you such captives for ancient oboli.

entries.title: Braga Initial Greeting
entries.text:
What brings you to this gods-cursed corner of the world, Exile?

Behold, the pride of Cimmeria, a clan of soft-minded fools who think they’ve died and gone to Crom’s realm.

Never mind that we walked through a desert to get here.

Never mind that we can still take pleasure and pain in equal measure.

Oh no, we must be in the afterlife.

Because it’s foggy.

entries.title: Braga Greeting
entries.text:
Yes?

We’re still here, Exile.

The wights haven’t taken us yet.

entries.title: Braga Farewell
entries.text:
Good luck on your journey.

Enjoy not being stuck in a gods-cursed muckhole.

Beware the old battlefield. The wights are dangerous.

entries.title: Braga Conversation 1
entries.text:
I don’t know how much help I can be to you, stranger.

I can’t even seem to convince my kinsmen to give up their delusion.

I feel like I must stay here – the goat-livered idiots in this clan are still my kin.

And there are the wights of course.

It’s an endless battle to retain my sanity.

entries.title: Braga Conversation 2
entries.text:
I came north with a companion, a Shemite who cut me from the cross.

He died on the road but before he did, we saw a great deal of this land.

Here, we have an ancient battlefield.

The crimson lotus that blossoms in the soil is proof that blood was once spilled in great quantities.

North, there is snow and ice, and a temple all a-glitter with frost and falling stars.

I have spoken with a strange Frost Giant who told me we were living in a dream.

In the Volcano I met a strange cult who claim to hear whispers from the ruins there.

They tried to stop me from leaving, but I left them to their delusions.

North is a Black Keep and the Silent Legion who guard it. I dared not stray too close.

That is the north.

entries.title: Braga Conversation 3
entries.text:
I have also ventured into the east.

First, I found my old enemies, the Aesir and Vanir,

now allied and calling themselves the Heirs of the North.

I passed by their village, where they herd mammoths and worship their frozen god.

On an island, I met a ghost who sang to me,

and though I did not understand her words, it made me weep.

I found a cave, which led to a passage, which led to a swamp,

where cruel and twisted creatures stalked the stagnant waters.

And still further east, I came to the shore of an ocean, or an inland sea.

Here, I spoke with pirates, building a ship to set them free.

These are the things I saw in the east.

entries.title: Braga Conversation 4
entries.text:
But west?

There is only one thing in the west.

A tomb built in to the side of the mountain, sealed with a huge door.

I tried to get inside, but there is some spell or curse which prevents it,

and I do not hold with witchcraft.

There was a dead man by the entrance –

perhaps his spirit would have tales to tell.

entries.title: Braga Conversation 5
entries.text:
I will stay here, and defend my people, stranger.

I hold out hope that they will eventually come to their senses,

and we can make a plan to escape this empty place.

But you, you have no obligation to me or my people.

Find a way to bring the cursewall down.

Free us all.

entries.title: Child Of Jhebbal Sag Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Come, come to me.

You have nothing to fear of me, human. I am simply lonely as I await my return to the misty lands.

I would like to talk.

entries.title: Child Of Jhebbal Sag Greeting
entries.text:
The dark moon wanes, human.

A loon whispered to me of your coming.

You return to this haunted grove.

entries.title: Child Of Jhebbal Sag Farewell
entries.text:
I will wander here, human, until I can return to the misty lands.

Your conversation shall be missed, human.

If I am gone when you return, know that I have returned whence I came.

entries.title: Child Of Jhebbal Sag Conversation 1
entries.text:
Is my appearance repellent to you, human? Your kind find no kinship in the unnatural.

My brother, Impisi, summoned me from the misty lands. We are bound by flesh, blood and our kinship with Jhebbal Sag.

But Impisi asked of me power that could not be given. He took my silence for disobedience and drove me forth from his camp in a wrath.

I simply wish to return to my realm, but Impisi will not allow it because he thinks I am withholding information.

entries.title: Child Of Jhebbal Sag Conversation 2
entries.text:
If I could simply kill Impisi to secure passage to my realm, I would do it.

But the incantations that he spoke bind us as one, tied together by invisible threads.

His thoughts are my thoughts; if he is struck, I am bruised. If I am cut, he bleeds.

There must be a severing of ties, if I am to find my way back.

entries.title: Child Of Jhebbal Sag Conversation 3
entries.text:
Do you know of Jhebbal Sag, human?

Once all living things worshipped him. When men and animals spoke the same tongue, he would walk among them in any form that he chose.

He would mate with females of all races and from those unions would birth half-divine children such as I. Such as Impsi.

Most men have forgotten the tongue that they shared with the animals. Only some few animals remember.

Would you learn to speak with the animals, human?

entries.title: Child Of Jhebbal Sag Conversation 4
entries.text:
The realm that I come from is volatile: a world of shifting energies and elemental forces.

By contrast, I find your world to be... tranquil.

But I suspect that my nature makes me more sensitive to the undercurrents that run beneath this strange land.

Take this grove, for instance. These trees were once something else. Not human, nor any other creature that I have encountered in this place.

And they are screaming. Endlessly screaming.

entries.title: Child Of Jhebbal Sag Conversation 5
entries.text:
Have you seen my brother, Impisi? He is more animal than man, lost in the mad-scented lust of the hunt.

When he summoned me, he sought the power to become a beast for a period. When I refused him and was outcast, he turned to darker powers.

I fear that a bat has flown over the Mountains of the Dead bearing his request to the Four Brothers of the Night.

What the gods of darkness have replied, I do not know.

But strange beasts stalk the night now, and once, I saw the shadow of the Children of Jhil passing over this place and into the mountains.

I fear what he has unleashed.

entries.title: Cimmerianchief Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Another soul enters Crom's perdition.

One by one, again and again.

There is always room for one more.

I am chieftain of these Cimmerians you see.

We have no quarrel with you, nor you with us,

for, you see, we are, all of us, already in hell.

entries.title: Cimmerianchief Greeting
entries.text:
You return. You will return again.

There is no leaving this place.

There is no escaping Crom's afterlife.

entries.title: Cimmerianchief Conversation 1
entries.text:
I'll say it plain. You call these the Exiled Lands, but we know what they truly are.

We are, all of us, dead. We are, all of us, doomed.

These are the endless days of tribulation.

We have sunk past the fathoming of hope.

We dwell in this land of doleful shades and wretchedness.

Stranger, you are in the underworld, the afterlife of Crom.

entries.title: Cimmerianchief Conversation 2
entries.text:
We dwell here at the Mounds of the Dead. An ancient, forgotten battlefield.

The soil never quite dried from all the blood it drank.

Every night, the shambling corpses rise. Gaunt ghoulish wights.

They must have done something truly heinous to earn this damnation.

I do not know what we did to earn our lesser damnation.

Every night the wights rise, and every night we slay them.

That is Crom's afterlife.

Dreary, eternal battle without glory or joy or reason.

We accept this dread burden. We abide.

entries.title: Cimmerianchief Conversation 3
entries.text:
Crom is our gloomy, grim god.

Chieftain of our pantheon, as I am chieftain of these Cimmerians.

We do not pray to Crom. It is best not to attract his attention,

for his hands only dole out doom and ruin.

Crom gives us one gift at birth.

He grants us the power to strive and slay.

Crom approves of courage and tenacity, even when in vain.

In that regard, we are his morose champions.

entries.title: conan Initial Greeting
entries.text:
So, you live. And we meet again.

Tell me, have you had a good chance to look around? Seen your fill of these Exiled Lands.

It’s not too late to get back on that crucifix!

entries.title: conan Greeting
entries.text:
Still walking, dead one?

Aye?

Can’t a man drink cheap wine in peace?

entries.title: conan Farewell
entries.text:
Walk warily, my twice-dead friend.

I’ll be here until the wine runs out.

Try not to get killed.

entries.title: conan Conversation 1
entries.text:
Any fool can see that you need answers.

Left hanging for the vultures on that cross. A cursed bracelet that keeps you trapped in this place.

Everybody trying to kill each other.

I should leave you all to your fate. I don’t have the stomach for sorcery.

Give me a foe that I can strike with cold iron!

Bah! I’ll be here a while.

This wine tastes like boiled camel’s piss and the bread’s half-weevil, but I’m comfortable enough.

If you have questions… ask.

entries.title: conan Conversation 2
entries.text:
I wandered for a while after I pulled you from that cross.

In the south, I found desperate souls and Darfari man-eaters.

In the east, apes that walked like men among the overgrown ruins of an ancient city.

I found an amphitheatre where the ancient dead walked and the memories of ancient battles stained the sand.

It stank of sorcery. So I left.

entries.title: conan Conversation 3
entries.text:
I have travelled far and seen much.

From the glittering ice of Asgard to the jungle expanses of the Black Kingdoms,

I have been thief, pirate and mercenary.

But nowhere have I seen gods as I see them in this land.

These avatars that stalk and spread destruction in their wake.

These are not gods. These are what men want the gods to be. Weapons.

entries.title: conan Conversation 4
entries.text:
There is a woman. I freed her from the cross, just as I did you.

I’ve followed her trail across the Exiled Lands. I missed her here, by a scant few hours.

She had left in search of a map room and a way to free herself from the Exiled Lands.

Her trail ended in the Map Room. She was gone and all her companions, slaughtered.

Listen! If you have news of Razma of Shem… Let me know.

entries.title: conan Conversation 5
entries.text:
What do I know of sorcerers and their feuds? I do not dabble in the dark arts.

But whoever, and whatever, put you here is a wielder of the dark arts.

You are like a wolf in a trap. You struggle to flee and yet, in the end it will kill you.

Be smarter. Find out what they want.

Then hold it close. They will come for it and then…

Your jaws around their throat.

entries.title: conan Conversation 6
entries.text:
Just remember this.

If you do succeed, if you do manage to find a way to remove your bracelet and leave the Exiled Lands.

You will have to make a choice.

Stay, and be the hero of your own story, or leave, and risk losing it all?

The choice will be yours.

Choose wisely.

entries.title: Despairing Writer Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Good day, friend! Perhaps you might be of service to me...

Fill this trough with raw meat and the beast will surely come.

entries.title: Despairing Writer Greeting
entries.text:
Good day, friend! Perhaps you might be of service to me...

Fill this trough with raw meat and the beast will surely come.

entries.title: Despairing Writer Farewell
entries.text: Farewell, fellow scholar.

entries.title: Despairing Writer Conversation 1
entries.text: The monoliths in this area tell the story of a bygone age. But they are crumbling.

entries.title: Despairing Writer Conversation 2
entries.text: Gather rubbings from them and deliver them to me, so that I may save their stories.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL F Initial Greeting
entries.text: His signs are nigh!
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL F
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL F

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL F Greeting
entries.text:
His signs are nigh!

His signs are nigh!

His signs are nigh!
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL F
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL F

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL F Farewell
entries.text:
Hunter or hunted, you are blessed by Jhebbal Sag!

Hunter or hunted, you are blessed by Jhebbal Sag!
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL F
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL F

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL F Conversation 1
entries.text:
Hearken to the whisper at the back of the skull, where reptiles swim in the primal soup.

A vague memory rises... a name... an ancient god of darkness and primordial fear,

to whom once both men and beasts bowed and whose children still lurk in the dark corners of the world.
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL F
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL F

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL F Conversation 2
entries.text:
Read His name, raggedy and red. Jhebbal Sag!

Read His name scribed upon the flesh of prey. Jhebbal Sag!
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL F
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL F

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL F Conversation 3
entries.text:
Have you seen His signs? In the long ago, the Prince of Beasts ruled all animals,

including humankind, and all creatures communed in a common tongue.
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL F
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL F

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL F Conversation 4
entries.text:
He once ruled where newer gods now reign. He will reign again, when the other gods fall.

His return is nigh!
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL F
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL F

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL F Conversation 5
entries.text:
The faithful gather and make ready for the sacrament of the hunt. The faithful compete to be His champion.

Seek you the gathering?
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL F
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL F

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL F Conversation 6
entries.text: Look north of the Nameless City, east of the Shattered Springs, south west of the Den.
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL F
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL F

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL M Initial Greeting
entries.text: His signs are nigh!
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL M
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL M

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL M Greeting
entries.text:
His signs are nigh!

His signs are nigh!

His signs are nigh!
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL M
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL M

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL M Farewell
entries.text: Hunter or hunted, you are blessed by Jhebbal Sag!
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL M
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL M

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL M Conversation 1
entries.text:
Hearken to the whisper at the back of the skull, where reptiles swim in the primal soup.

A vague memory rises... a name... an ancient god of darkness and primordial fear,

to whom once both men and beasts bowed and whose children still lurk in the dark corners of the world.
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL M
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL M

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL M Conversation 2
entries.text:
Read His name, raggedy and red. Jhebbal Sag!

Read His name scribed upon the flesh of prey. Jhebbal Sag!
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL M
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL M

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL M Conversation 3
entries.text:
Have you seen His signs? In the long ago, the Prince of Beasts ruled all animals,

including humankind, and all creatures communed in a common tongue.
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL M
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL M

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL M Conversation 4
entries.text:
He once ruled where newer gods now reign. He will reign again, when the other gods fall.

His return is nigh!
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL M
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL M

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL M Conversation 5
entries.text:
The faithful gather and make ready for the sacrament of the hunt. The faithful compete to be His champion.

Seek you the gathering?
entries.groupLabel: Event Gods JS Herald EL M
entries.speakerName: Event Gods JS Herald EL M

entries.title: Event Gods JS Herald EL M Conversation 6
entries.text: Look north of the Nameless City, east of the Shattered Springs, south west of the Den.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest EL Initial Greeting
entries.text: Another pilgrim enters the savage garden...

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest EL Greeting
entries.text: Predator or prey, you are welcome in the sacred hunt.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest EL Farewell
entries.text: His scripture is written, raggedy and red, in fang and claw.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest EL Conversation 1
entries.text:
Before the before... Jhebbal Sag reined over all the earth, over all beasts, including humankind.

All predators and prey worshipped Him and spoke the same language.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest EL Conversation 2
entries.text:
Even now, separated by ten thousand tongues... Even now, when the corruption of civilization peaks,

the god of all beasts walks among us, sometimes as a man, sometimes as an animal,

and you will know him by eyes that burn like yellow coals in the dark.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest EL Conversation 3
entries.text:
The smartest of animals, who remember the beginning of all things, still worship him,

as do the men and women who still feel the primal fever, the quickened heart, the blood-soaked drum of the hunt.

Animal and human, they are descendants of Jhebbal Sag's loins!

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest EL Conversation 4
entries.text:
The hunt is Jhebbal Sag's most sacred sacrament. We hunt for His glory.

In this time of the gathering, we hunt to be his next chosen.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest EL Conversation 5
entries.text:
Perhaps you have already faced the ferocity of Jhebbal Sag's chosen. Those most devoted to His hunt lope in the darkness after the horizon has devoured the sun,

far from the blasphemy of civilization's walls. Slay these chosen for the trophies that will buy you His favor.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest EL Conversation 6
entries.text:
Search the lands for His champions three: the mammoth guarding a frozen temple

the rhino in the shadow of a sea vessel locked in the sands, and the panther on the shores to the east.

Each champion a vicious paragon of its kind.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest EL Conversation 7
entries.text:
Seek a Mark of Jhebbal Sag, set a lure, and draw a champion to you. To properly consecrate the combat,

strip yourself of the obscenity of armor and adorn yourself with the painted sigils of the Beast Lord.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest EL Conversation 8
entries.text:
From the flesh of these champions, carve and take the brand that marks them as Jhebbal Sag's.

Combine their holy flesh with a lure upon this altar, drink the nectar we have prepared, and ready your soul

for an audience with King of Beast's greatest champion!

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest EL Conversation 9
entries.text: The Master of the Hunt abides in this encampment and can supply you with all you shall need in the holy hunt.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest Siptah Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Come, come to me.

You have nothing to fear of me, human. I am simply lonely as I await my return to the misty lands.

I would like to talk.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest Siptah Greeting
entries.text:
The dark moon wanes, human.

A loon whispered to me of your coming.

You return to this haunted grove.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Priest Siptah Farewell
entries.text:
I will wander here, human, until I can return to the misty lands.

Your conversation shall be missed, human.

If I am gone when you return, know that I have returned whence I came.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Initial Greeting
entries.text: I know a hunter when I see one.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Greeting
entries.text: A little respite between kills?

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Farewell
entries.text:
Howls on the wind. Blood on the tongue.

Howls on the wind. Blood on the tongue.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Conversation 1
entries.text:
Give me a wild place. Give me wind whispering deadly secrets in the branches. Give me a gravid moon

hanging heavy in a hungry sky, setting my teeth on edge and my toes curling in the clay of blood and soil...

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Conversation 2
entries.text:
That being said... I'm rather enjoying this warm place to rest and recover.

That being said... I'm rather enjoying this warm place to rest and recover.

I feel a vague ache and weariness... perhaps it is the climate. I am a stranger in a strange land.

I feel a vague ache and weariness... perhaps it is the climate. I am a stranger in a strange land.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Conversation 3
entries.text: No matter. Let us indulge in libation and palaver.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Conversation 4
entries.text:
You have a hunger about you...

...a hunger not alone of belly-emptiness, but the lust of death-dealing.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Conversation 5
entries.text:
I wonder... I wonder if you are on the hunt.

I wonder if you seek the sacraments of Jhebbal Sag, to slay his champions and claim his blessing.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Conversation 6
entries.text:
You are no scavenger, no jackal.

You aspire to the apex. You desire the Beast Lord's greatest champion.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Conversation 7
entries.text:
Yes, this current grand champion of Jhebbal Sag is not quite right, are they?

Too powerful. Some dark magic possesses them, yes?

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Conversation 8
entries.text:
I wonder... I wonder at the nature of gods, how growing divinity turns them into a multiplicity --

how each of their extremities might gain agency, like a worm's cloven head becoming two...

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Conversation 9
entries.text: Hahahaha...I think these pleasant libations have fogged my mind with philosophizing... though not unpleasantly so.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Conversation 10
entries.text: No doubt, others have offered you advice on how to deal with this corrupted champion. I offer an alternative.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Conversation 11
entries.text: A predator should not only be mighty, but cunning. You could face this possessed champion head on, or you could cleanse it of the corrupting influence.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Conversation 12
entries.text:
In a dark, forgotten place, seek out a primeval hyena who walks as a man, one of Jhebbal Sag's first champions.

Lure this beast out of its ancient slumber. Use its elder blood to further season your lures.

Consuming this mixture will purge the corrupted champion.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Stranger Conversation 13
entries.text: I have enjoyed our conversation, in this cozy watering hole. I wish you success on your hunt.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Vendor Initial Greeting
entries.text: Lovely weather for a hunt, eh?

entries.title: Event Gods JS Vendor Greeting
entries.text:
Welcome, hunter.

Welcome, hunter.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Vendor Farewell
entries.text: Happy hunting.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Vendor Conversation 1
entries.text:
Bring me offerings harvested from the hunt, to honor the Prince of Beasts, Jhebbal Sag. In return, I will give goods to aid your hunts.

Defeat Jhebbal Sag's nocturnal hunters and champions -- collect them for the trophies that bring His favor.

entries.title: Event Gods JS Vendor Conversation 2
entries.text:
Jhebbal Sag is partial to teeth. Ah yes, the teeth, the teeth!

Little ivory affirmations of our savage joy. Bring me the teeth!

entries.title: Femalemerchant1 Initial Greeting
entries.text:
What can I get you?

Buy something, I haven't got all day!

I'm certain I've got whatever you need.

You buying?

A good day for a good trade.

My stock is limited, so buy now.

You can walk out of here with a bargain, or a frown. Your choice.

entries.title: Femalemerchant1 Greeting
entries.text:
What can I get you?

Buy something, I haven't got all day!

I'm certain I've got whatever you need.

You buying?

A good day for a good trade.

My stock is limited, so buy now.

You can walk out of here with a bargain, or a frown. Your choice.

entries.title: Femalemerchant1 Farewell
entries.text:
No refunds!

Pleasure doing business with you.

Come again, if you survive.

I'll have more in stock tomorrow.

Come back anytime.

Come and see me again when you have more gold to spend.

Farewell, Exile.

Safe travels, Exile.

May Set guide your steps.

entries.title: Femalethrall1 Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Help…please.

I'll help you. Just get me out of here.

Rescue me from this, and I will follow you anywhere.

Don't leave me here.

The gods have sent you to save me.

Don't turn your back on me, please.

Give me freedom and I will serve you.

Don't let them kill me. If I live, I can help you.

Please… I can work for you.

Give me a chance. I'll do anything.

Don't leave me here.

The gods have sent you to save me.

Don't turn your back on me, please.

Give me freedom and I will serve you.

Don't let them kill me. If I live, I can help you.

Please… I can work for you.

entries.title: Freya Angvarr Intro 1
entries.text: Angvarr it's me, don't you recognize me?

entries.title: Freya Angvarr Intro 3
entries.text: For the love of Ymir, Angvarr. It's your wife, Freya.

entries.title: Freya Angvarr Intro 5
entries.text: Aaaah! Kill her!

entries.title: Freya Hulda Confrontation 1
entries.text: I can feel your darkness. your curse. Why did you do this to me, Hulda?

entries.title: Freya Hulda Confrontation 3
entries.text: So it wasn't about Angvarr at all? You just wanted to ruin what I have? To curse me for being who I am?

entries.title: Freya Hulda Confrontation 5
entries.text: I can feel...the darkness. My wound... her curse... stop it. Don't let her take my mind.

entries.title: Freya Hulda Confrontation 7
entries.text: Enough... enough. The clarity of pain. You taught me how to fight through my weakness, HULDA! The Valkyrie has returned!

entries.title: Freya Hulda Confrontation Victory
entries.text: She's dead. At last I can feel the taint of her sliding off my soul.

entries.title: Freya Campfire Prayer 1
entries.text: I'm going to try a prayer to Ymir. You can watch, but don't interrupt.

entries.title: Freya Campfire Prayer 2
entries.text: Oh, Gods of ice and snow, Heed my strength. By my arm, were these creatures slain. By my will, were these creatures taken.

entries.title: Freya Campfire Prayer 3
entries.text: By bone and right of northern blood, I beseech thee. Answer this prayer. Whither goes my strength?

entries.title: Freya Campfire Prayer 4
entries.text: Do you see it? A figure in the smoke. A woman - I know her! It is the witch, Hulda! I see her holding my sword.

entries.title: Freya Near Ingredient
entries.text: I see one of the plants I need. This way!

entries.title: Freya Near Ingredient
entries.text: There's one of the ingredients I need.

entries.title: Freya Near Ingredient
entries.text: Ah yes, another ingredient.

entries.title: Freya Collect Ingredient
entries.text: Got it.

entries.title: Freya Collect Ingredient
entries.text: I have it, let's keep moving.

entries.title: Freya Collect Ingredient
entries.text: There we go, got it.

entries.title: Freya Finish Medium Hunt Animals
entries.text: Agh! My arm aches - that's enough cutting for now.

entries.title: Freya Finish All Medium Hunt
entries.text: I think... I think it is time to go back and rest.

entries.title: Freya Near Wolf Location
entries.text: Shhh, do you hear it? The tread of padded feet? There's an ancient wolf nearby. Follow me.

entries.title: Freya Near Wolf Location
entries.text: Shhh, do you hear it? The tread of padded feet? There's an ancient wolf nearby. Follow me.

entries.title: Freya Near Elk Location
entries.text: There's something just downwind of here. An elk, I can smell it - luckily it can't scent us yet. Stay low and follow me.

entries.title: Freya Near Bear Location
entries.text: I can see the spoor of an ancient bear. Let's track it. This way.

entries.title: Freya Near Boar Location
entries.text: Something had disturbed the ground here. A boar, and from the look of it, a big one. Let's go carefully.

entries.title: Freya Finish Big Hunt
entries.text: That's enough hunting. I feel... good. My wound is still pulling, but the fog in my mind seems to have passed.

entries.title: Freya Biome Southern River
entries.text: The water here is clear as the glacier streams from home. Not as icy.

entries.title: Freya Biome Jungle
entries.text: I dislike this place, trees should not grow this large.

entries.title: Freya Biome Savannah
entries.text: I've never seen grasslands like this. The moors and tundra of home offer less concealment.

entries.title: Freya Biome Highlands
entries.text: These lands remind me of home, in the summer time.

entries.title: Freya Biome Tundra
entries.text: A dreary place. Well suited to the Cimmerians. Grey and humorless.

entries.title: Freya Biome Snow
entries.text: I miss the sparkle of sun on the snow. The weak suns of winter. The exiled lands cannot compare to Asgard.

entries.title: Freya Dungeon Dregs
entries.text: Be wary! I mistrust this hole in the Earth. And it stinks of the sewers.

entries.title: Freya Dungeon Black Keep
entries.text: The skulls tell fearsome tales of this place. If we enter, we may not return.

entries.title: Freya Dungeon Kurak
entries.text: The only good Sorcerer is a dead Sorcerer. Present company accepted, of course...

entries.title: Freya Dungeon Silver Mine
entries.text: Caution! This place makes my skin crawl...

entries.title: Freya Dungeon Dagon
entries.text: I wonder what lies beneath these waves...buried in the deeps...

entries.title: Freya Dungeon Well Of Skelos
entries.text: A cursed place this must be, to be buried at the heart of a fiery lake...

entries.title: Freya Dungeon Warmakers
entries.text: The ancients left their mark all over these lands. This place is one of theirs...

entries.title: Freya Dungeon Wine Cellar
entries.text: Men never learn, we just build on top of our old mistakes and make them again.

entries.title: Freya Capital Summoning Place
entries.text: Foul cannibals! This place stinks of blood and sacrifice.

entries.title: Freya Capital New Asagarth
entries.text: For a while, this was my home. But these are no longer my kin.

entries.title: Freya Capital Mounds Of The Dead
entries.text: The mad Cimmerians, you cannot reason with them. They think they are dead.

entries.title: Freya Capital Sepermeru
entries.text: Thieves, slavers, murderers... and the only place in these Exiled Lands where they dont kill strangers.

entries.title: Freya Capital The Den
entries.text: This place smells like a kennel.

entries.title: Freya Capital Sinners Refuge
entries.text: At least some of us made it. This place is for the ones who didn't.

entries.title: Freya Capital Black Galleon
entries.text: It's an impressive effort, carving a ship from stone. The unique madness of the Exiled Lands at work.

entries.title: Freya Capital Unnamed City
entries.text: The very Earth is poisoned here. An evil place...

entries.title: Freya Capital Temple Of Frost
entries.text: This place feel sacrosanct. We would do well to tread carefully. Lest we raise the wrath of Ymir

entries.title: Freya Capital Xel Ha
entries.text: The fetid stink of the past clings to these ruins. Best be done with our business and leave.

entries.title: Freya Egg Overwatch
entries.text: As a child in Vanneheim i used to play blizzard games with my friend Gavin. But then Gavin got taken by the blizzard.

entries.title: Freya POISinkhole
entries.text: An old arena... bloodsport never goes out of fashion...

entries.title: Freya POITeliths Island
entries.text: The Exiled Lands has moments of beauty. Places like this.

entries.title: Freya POIShaleback Hollow
entries.text: Aye, the stench of corruption clings strongly here. Be wary that it doesn't taint your soul.

entries.title: Freya POICircle Of Swords
entries.text: There is a sadness to this place. Something beautiful was broken here, I can feel it...

entries.title: Freya POIThe Passage
entries.text: I don't like these tunnels beneath the Earth. Give me the open sky.

entries.title: Freya POIGodsclaw Passage
entries.text: Who built this? Why? So much effort only to fall into darkness and decay...

entries.title: Freya POIHighway
entries.text: Any cave guarded by a fearsome beast is to be approached warily...

entries.title: Freya POIDragonmouth
entries.text: Through the mouth and into the belly of the dragon. A belly full of fire.

entries.title: Freya POIGreat Dam
entries.text: This dam, even the shining cities of Aquilonia do not have stone work such as this...

entries.title: Freya POIRuins Of Xullan
entries.text: The cursed dead stalk these ruins... there is nothing here for the living...

entries.title: Freya POIHand Of The Maker
entries.text: I heard tell of a traveller who camped on the hand... it crushed them during the night.

entries.title: Freya POIHand Of The Maker
entries.text: Beware the toxic air and cover your mouth and nose... the smell will kill you if the toxins don't...

entries.title: Freya POIBridge Of The Betrayer
entries.text: Walk carefully... who knows how these ancient stones might shift...

entries.title: Freya POIBarrow King
entries.text: The tomb of an ancient horror, opened by blood... we won't like what we find...

entries.title: Freya POISepermeru Tavern
entries.text: Aahh... time to slake our thirst and kick the dust of the Exiled Lands from our boots.

entries.title: Freya POIJihl Roost
entries.text: Bird shit and battle? Why do I follow you into places like this?

entries.title: Gilzan The Treasure Hunter Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Good day, noble adventurer

I am Gilzan, treasure hunter and man of fortune.

If you are planning to wander down the avenues of this cursed city, then perhaps I can offer some advice.

No charge the first time.

entries.title: Gilzan The Treasure Hunter Greeting
entries.text:
Back again?

What do you need?

How can I advise you?

entries.title: Gilzan The Treasure Hunter Farewell
entries.text:
Fortune favour you.

May your pouch overflow with gold.

Be careful out there. It's hard to know who to trust.

entries.title: Gilzan The Treasure Hunter Conversation 1
entries.text:
You happened along at the right moment, friend.

A grand opportunity awaits in the city, if you can only find the right chamber.

I've marked it for you with the sign of the serpent. You'll know you're in the right place when you see it.

Just follow the passage. Great treasure awaits.

entries.title: Gilzan The Treasure Hunter Conversation 2
entries.text:
You might be wondering why I decided to just share the location of that treasure with you?

I just have a generous, trusting nature, is all. I didn't come here as an exile like so many of you others.

Nah, I volunteered. They were hiring people like me to help them find relics in the ruins.

Of course, they told me the bracelet was to stop me running away with any relics I found. They didn't tell me it would make my head explode.

Lucky for me, I pushed that other fool over the boundary to test it first.

entries.title: Gilzan The Treasure Hunter2 Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Good day, noble adventurer

I am Gilzan, treasure hunter and man of fortune.

If you are planning to wander down the avenues of this cursed city, then perhaps I can offer some advice.

No charge the first time.

entries.title: Gilzan The Treasure Hunter2 Greeting
entries.text:
Back again?

What do you need?

How can I advise you?

entries.title: Gilzan The Treasure Hunter2 Farewell
entries.text:
Fortune favour you.

May your pouch overflow with gold.

Be careful out there. It's hard to know who to trust.

entries.title: Gilzan The Treasure Hunter2 Conversation 1
entries.text:
Look, I don't know exactly what this place is, but a lot of things here don't add up.

First, a lot of people who should be dead are alive in here. Why were they spared?

Second, the purge. They kill anybody and anything that gets in their path, yes, but they also take any relics that they can find.

Some of the less reputable people I know call this "skimming". You let some other fools take all the risk and then rob them blind after it is done.

These Exiled Lands are one hell of a scam.

entries.title: Gilzan The Treasure Hunter2 Conversation 2
entries.text:
It seems that the relics are the entire point of this whole exercise.

Whoever or whatever it was that built the ruins - it's them that the people who created this place are interested in.

We're just here to be cheap labour. But that's what gives us an opportunity.

If you can figure out what they're looking for, and you can find it first, and you can defend it from the Purge... Well then you'll have the biggest bargaining chip of all.

And then you can cut a deal.

entries.title: Hulda Intro
entries.text: I told you, Freya. Angvarr belongs to me now. Body and soul.

entries.title: Hulda Angvarr Intro 4
entries.text: Choose, Freya. Your husband, or your life?

entries.title: Hulda Confrontation 2
entries.text: Why? Because you were the one who could fight. You were the one he loved. You were the Valkyrie. And I was always just "The Witch".

entries.title: Hulda Confrontation 4
entries.text: No. I wanted to control you.

entries.title: Hulda Confrontation 6
entries.text: What? Nooo!

entries.title: Jamila The Pirate Queen Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Watch your shadow, cully.

You've not the saltwalter stagger of the regular fools who come seeking the blade of the Butcher of Khwarizm.

So we'll talk. If you keep your distance.

entries.title: Jamila The Pirate Queen Greeting
entries.text:
What tidings, traveler?

Ahoy Exile! What news?

You're back. Again.

entries.title: Jamila The Pirate Queen Farewell
entries.text:
Keep yourself safe.

May the wind guide you, exile.

May you always find water, wanderer.

entries.title: Jamila The Pirate Queen Conversation 1
entries.text:
Come to gawk at the captain who lost her ship and her crew?

Twice the captain and twice the exile now, thanks to those mutinous dogs.

I put the salt back in their blood and this is how they repay me?

Treacherous bastards. The Black Hand only exists because of me!

entries.title: Jamila The Pirate Queen Conversation 2
entries.text:
When I was exiled here, like the others, I was adrift, directionless.

They burned my hands before they cut me loose, burned them into the twisted flesh lumps you see today.

I had to learn everything anew. How to eat, how to fight. How to survive.

Make no mistake, exile, this place is a crucible. We are being shaped and moulded to some malevolent plan.

entries.title: Jamila The Pirate Queen Conversation 3
entries.text:
On the Vilayet, I always knew our heading.

There was always another Turanian sail to burn, another fortune to be won.

But here, I needed a new course.

There were rogues aplenty - Barrachans, Zingaran Freebooters and members of the Red Brotherhood, exiles all.

I gathered them together and I bound them with a promise. If we could work together, I would find us a way back to the ocean.

That was how the Black Hand began...

entries.title: Jamila The Pirate Queen Conversation 4
entries.text:
The rules of the Red Brotherhood allow any crew member to challenge the captain.

I tried to keep the same tradition in the Black Hand. Any who could best me, could take my position.

Mutinous cowards didn't even try. They just waited until I was asleep, then chained me to the hull.

Never trust a pirate.

entries.title: Jamila The Pirate Queen Conversation 5
entries.text:
Look, I don't really do treasure maps, as rule. But there is something in the ruins of that city.

A room, but the floor looks like a map. And embedded in that floor are gems.

My bracelet was glowing like witchfire and all of the gems on the floor were lit up to match it.

I don't hold with sorcery, so I got my crew out of there. After we tried to pry up a few of the gems, naturally.

entries.title: Jamila The Pirate Queen2 Initial Greeting
entries.text:
I swore I'd make it back to the salt water, did I not?

Well here we are, old friend. A ship, a crew and an ocean to explore. Captain once again.

Now, what trouble brings you to the lost sea?

entries.title: Jamila The Pirate Queen2 Greeting
entries.text:
What tidings, traveler?

Ahoy Exile! What news?

You're back. Again.

entries.title: Jamila The Pirate Queen2 Farewell
entries.text:
Keep yourself safe.

May the wind guide you, exile.

May you always find water, wanderer.

entries.title: Jamila The Pirate Queen2 Conversation 1
entries.text:
We've been to the islands out there. There are ruins on some of them, but not like the ruins we found in the desert.

To start with, these ones are human sized. I think there used to be people living here.

And there is still a line of those bloody pillars out there, so we can't see what is on the other side.

I shouldn't complain, the gods led me back to the saltwater. And that is all I ever wanted.

entries.title: Lone Hunter Initial Greeting
entries.text:
A fellow hunter! Perhaps you could help lure the beast to me.

Fill this trough with raw meat and the beast will surely come.

entries.title: Lone Hunter Greeting
entries.text:
A fellow hunter! Perhaps you could help lure the beast to me.

Fill this trough with raw meat and the beast will surely come.

entries.title: Lone Hunter Farewell
entries.text: Farewell, hunter, and may your eyes stay true.

entries.title: Lone Hunter Conversation 1
entries.text: I have tracked the beast for almost four days. It is close - I can feel it.

entries.title: Lone Hunter Conversation 2
entries.text: Fill this trough with raw meat and the beast will surely come.

entries.text:
What'll it be?

What can I get you?

Buy something, I haven't got all day!

I'm certain I've got whatever you need.

You can walk out of here with a bargain, or a frown. Your choice.

entries.title: Mecenary Captain Conversation 1
entries.text:
Serpents and stars! Freedom and a good blade are the only things I hold sacred. You've won my favor if not my friendship.

What can I do for you in turn? I can sell you a banner under which my mercenaries will unite to defend you and yours.

What's more, I can sell you war supplies -- food, siege equipment, and the like -- all at discount I wouldn't offer my own brother. He's a drunk and a fiend... but that is another story.

I'm as grizzled as they come. I've got a long history, written in blood, helping kings and princes win their little wars.

I'm as grizzled as they come. I've got a long history, written in blood, helping kings and princes win their little wars.

I was once a member of Zarallo's Free Companions. Heard of them? Of course you have!

I was once a member of Zarallo's Free Companions. Heard of them? Of course you have!

But eventually, I struck out on my own. Gathered my closest siblings in slaughter under my own flag. We slinked away in the night. For greater glory! Haven't found it yet... but it's the journey. Ha!

entries.title: Mek-kamoses A2 C1 Initial Greeting
entries.text: Greetings, Exile.

entries.title: Mek-kamoses A2 C1 Greeting
entries.text: I've been expecting you.

entries.title: Mek-kamoses A2 C1 Farewell
entries.text: We'll meet again. I have foreseen it.

entries.title: Mek-kamoses A2 C1 Conversation 1
entries.text: The loss of a body is a small matter for one who commands the obeisance of many other bodies.

entries.title: Mek-kamoses A2 C1 Conversation 2
entries.text: By and by, I shall bide my time, and by my so potent art, make myself whole again.

entries.title: Mek-kamoses S1 C3 Initial Greeting
entries.text:
I've been expecting you.

I've been expecting you.

Greetings, Exile.

Greetings, Exile.

My plan’s near fruition...

entries.text:
I've been expecting you.

Greetings, Exile.

entries.title: Mek-kamoses S1 C3 Farewell
entries.text:
We'll meet again. I have foreseen it.

We'll meet again. I have foreseen it.

Away now. There is much to attend to.

Away now. There is much to attend to.

Farewell, Exile.

Farewell, Exile.

entries.title: Mek-kamoses S1 C3 Conversation 1
entries.text:
The visions came to me, wafting like lotus smoke. The harder I grasped, the more the truths eluded the grip of my mind.

I had to be very still, melt away all notion of self, and stalk those cagey revelations like the ghost of a thought.

entries.title: Mek-kamoses S1 C3 Conversation 2
entries.text: By steaming entrails and divination dripping on a plate, I saw the end of the Exiled Lands. A hungry doom ready to swallow us all.

entries.title: Mek-kamoses S1 C3 Conversation 3
entries.text:
But I also saw a way out! By my so potent art, I can harness the coming eclipse and subvert Kurak's workings.

Through his madness, he taps into potent forces, true, but his strength is also his undoing.

entries.title: Mek-kamoses S1 C3 Conversation 4
entries.text:
Kurak is chaos, a tangled knot of a thousand serpent heads devouring a thousand tails.

I, on the other hand, am precision.

I am control.

entries.title: Mek-kamoses S1 C3 Conversation 5
entries.text: The preparations are made. The altar is ready. When the moon eclipses the sun, I will harness the power of the celestial alignment.

entries.title: Mek-kamoses S1 C3 Conversation 6
entries.text:
I only require more blood crystals... and the head of Kurak. Through my visions, a spied a way to tap into Kurak's Transportory stone.

It is the only way in and out of his lair. Away now, before all is lost.

entries.title: Mountaineer Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Do not let me spoil this moment for you stranger.

To conquer a peak such as this, is no small thing.

entries.title: Mountaineer Greeting
entries.text:
Again, you climb to the highest peak. It is thrilling, yes?

The air is simply better this high up, don’t you agree?

Again, you climb to the highest peak. It is thrilling, yes?

The air is simply better this high up, don’t you agree?

entries.title: Mountaineer Farewell
entries.text:
May your grip be always strong, friend.

Never lose your love of heights, friend.

May your grip be always strong, friend.

Never lose your love of heights, friend.

entries.title: Mountaineer Conversation 1
entries.text:
You saw a peak and had to climb it? I know the appeal.

It is freedom, in a place where we have so little.

The world is a beautiful place from the top of a mountain.

So far above the corruption and blood and darkness that has seeped into the ground of this land.

I know. You have better things to do than philosophize up here with me.

Take your leave friend.

Just remember that you can always climb away from your troubles.

entries.title: Muriela Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Blessings of Mitra upon you, pilgrim.

Are you come to this land to seek atonement for the sins of your past life?

Perhaps not, no.

You have the haunted look of one who seeks dominion or escape, or both.

Those paths are dead ends, pilgrim. The path of Mitra is the one, true path.

entries.title: Muriela Greeting
entries.text: Yes, pilgrim?

entries.title: Muriela Farewell
entries.text: Walk with Mitra, pilgrim.

entries.title: Muriela Conversation 1
entries.text:
Once, I was deaf to the words of the divine, pilgrim. Like you, I wandered aimless.

But Mitra chose me. Spoke to me. Offered me a purpose.

I did not understand the nature of the task I was chosen for. I still do not.

But that is faith, yes?

entries.title: Muriela Conversation 2
entries.text:
To invite perfection, is to create perfection. That is what I am building here.

A vessel for Mitra. A place for his divine essence to inhabit.

I do not know if I will succeed. The smallest flaw might have incredible consequences.

And so I work slowly. A hammer strike here, a chisel there.

What matter if the work consumes all the days of my life?

entries.title: Muriela Conversation 3
entries.text:
What do you know of Mitra, pilgrim?

He is perhaps a gentler, kinder god than those you have encountered in your homeland.

He abhors blood sacrifice and prefers simple, dignified rituals and the preservation of wisdom and knowledge.

In the old days, he was called the Defender, and he chose mortal champions to defend his chosen from the ancient demons.

Perhaps you are one such champion, simply waiting to be discovered.

Who can say?

entries.title: Muriela Conversation 4
entries.text:
There is great imbalance in this land.

Ancient wounds that have never healed and have been torn open anew.

These bracelets corrupt us, turning us into pawns of ancient evil.

I resist the voices. I unsee the ghosts when they appear.

But…I have a doubt.

Does the corruption infect my work?

Will it be Mitra who comes to this statue when it is complete, or something else entirely?

entries.title: Muriela Conversation 5
entries.text:
The last eyelid carved and then…

Can you feel it, pilgrim?

Mitra comes. My god shall walk.

entries.title: Mysterious Pilgrim Initial Greeting
entries.text: I have tracked the beast for almost four days. It is close - I can feel it.

entries.title: Mysterious Pilgrim Greeting
entries.text: I have tracked the beast for almost four days. It is close - I can feel it.

entries.text: I have tracked the beast for almost four days. It is close - I can feel it.

entries.title: Nunu The Cannibal Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Stay your hand, stranger. Come, look into the pit.

The Lord of Empty Abodes awaits.

I am his shaman, Nunu. Your coming was revealed to me in the whispering of a bat's wings.

entries.title: Nunu The Cannibal Greeting
entries.text:
You return. But have you brought the sacred flesh?

The fires await us all, Exile.

Yog whispered of your return, friend.

Flesh sobbing, ebbing, throbbing.

entries.title: Nunu The Cannibal Farewell
entries.text:
Yogs hunger is eternal, wanderer.

Listen to the whisperings. Yog knows you.

entries.title: Nunu The Cannibal Conversation 1
entries.text:
You are lost. Seeking guidance, yes?

Sometimes, the whispers are silent. My lord grows distant.

I weep alone in the desert. But there is a cure.

The smoke of the lotus.

It deafens the world so that the whisper of Yog becomes a roaring shout.

And the visions – they reveal to me the will of Yog.

If you need guidance, inhale the lotus.

The path will become clear.

entries.title: Nunu The Cannibal Conversation 2
entries.text:
The paths revealed to you by the lotus will not always be simple.

Every opportunity is a test and every test requires strength and an indomitable will.

You cannot simply wander into darkness and expect to conquer.

You will need to prepare, build yourself up.

And find the other lotus, they bloom in other places and have many colors.

I have a feeling, my friend, that you are going to taste them all before you are through.

entries.title: Nunu The Cannibal Conversation 3
entries.text:
My heart has a question. Do you know the Lord of Empty Abodes?

He knows you, child, through the flesh of your kind.

He hungers to know more.

All flesh is knowledge. Every savory thought.

Bring us knowledge of others. Yog is generous.

entries.title: Nunu The Cannibal Conversation 4
entries.text:
We are exiled without conviction. Our crimes are of no moment.

My crime was my belief. What matter the age of the flesh that my lord consumes?

Does the Lord of Empty Abodes make a distinction between flesh in the womb and flesh outside of it?

Now my tribe walks without a shaman. And I seek the pure flesh to feed my god.

entries.title: Nunu The Cannibal Conversation 5
entries.text:
My lord whispers to me of this place. He walked here in ages past.

Here there was a banquet of flesh for his pleasure. And kings called upon his wisdom.

It is men who destroyed that age of abundance. Yog urges us to seek atonement.

The flesh we bring is an atonement. A satiation of past sins.

entries.title: Outcast Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Hrmmm. By the gods of ice and snow, a new dream approaches.

Hello, little dream.

You are probably not real, but I offer you welcome and polite palaver.

You are exiled. As am I.

Philosopher giant, some call me.

I have been called sophist and fool.

I am the Outcast, and I no longer have a name.

entries.title: Outcast Farewell
entries.text: This is nothing but dreaming.

entries.title: Outcast Conversation 1
entries.text:
I have seen the hoar-frost glittering in the sun.

I have heard the wind whispering across the everlasting snows.

But with my mind's eye... I have seen the dark universe yawning.

I have seen the black planets that roll without meaning or purpose or name.

I apologize, little dream.

My kin found my philosophizing tedious.

And so they cast me out.

entries.title: Outcast Conversation 2
entries.text:
The frost giants, the children of Ymir, my kin -

they do not appreciate abstractions.

No one detested my musings so much as our leader, Hrugnir of the Frost.

Hrugnir was a creature of instinct.

He only existed in the immediate present.

Battle and blood and the steam rising off a slain enemy -

these were all he valued.

Hrugnir commanded that I cease spouting my philosophies,

cease even to think them. I could not.

Hrugnir stripped me of my name.

He beat my skull until the fragmented bones avalanched over my eyes.

When I awoke, I was banished.

In banishment I remain.

entries.title: Outcast Conversation 3
entries.text:
I do not believe the Exiled Lands are real.

Do you laugh? Do you think this statement mad?

Let us discuss.

I submit to you this first point:

This place does not quite make sense.

It nearly makes sense, but there are strange inconsistencies.

At first I thought the Exiled Lands were a purgatory.

Hrugnir, I thought, must have killed me.

But then I revised my position.

The Exiled Lands are a dream. Everything is figment.

We are puppets to the bidding of vast, formless things from beyond.

Do you not believe me?

Let me ask you, have you dreamed in this place? I have not.

You cannot dream within a dream.

entries.title: Outcast Conversation 4
entries.text:
Now, little dream, I offer this disturbing observation -

you are not real.

I do not jest. Think back, little dream.

Do you have any clear memories of a time before this place?

Can you form a single recollection from childhood? Were you ever even a child?

Or did you take form whole-cloth when the Exiled Lands blinked into existence?

Nothing is real. There is only empty, frigid space and you.

And you are but thought.

entries.title: Petruso Initial Greeting
entries.text:
A mad fool went wandering in a sandstorm.

Eventually, a man happened along and said

“You mad fool, what are you doing out in this sandstorm?”

The mad fool replied—

Your turn to talk fool!

entries.title: Petruso Greeting
entries.text:
Look out, it’s that mad person again.

Crazy fool, you went into the sandstorm again.

entries.title: Petruso Farewell
entries.text:
Stay out of the sandstorms, you great fool.

Beware of inclement weather.

Poor fool. I don’t expect them to survive long.

entries.title: Petruso Conversation 1
entries.text:
Singing sands, stinging sands,

Singing, stinging, wringing sands.

Fighting sands, biting sands,

Fighting, biting, smiting sands.

Exiled lands, reptile lands,

Exiled reptile defiled lands.

entries.title: Petruso Conversation 2
entries.text:
Seven prophets went a-walking,

Bearing all their tricks,

One was caught by Hanuman,

And soon t’was only six.

Six prophets went a-climbing,

To get above the hive,

One was stung by locusts wings,

And then t’were only five.

Five prophets went a-crawling,

Beneath the city floor,

One went mad with power,

And soon there were just four.

Four prophets went a-dancing,

Beneath the corpse-fruit tree,

One climbed up and stayed there,

Leaving just the three.

Three prophets went a-fighting,

Through the pirates crew,

One became a mutineer,

And soon there were just two.

Two prophets went a-wandering,

Beneath the burning sun,

One got lost in a sandstorm,

And left the other one.

One prophet went a-gathering,

The artefacts of yore,

He put them all together and—

Was never seen no more.

entries.title: Petruso Conversation 3
entries.text:
This is the best place to hear it, you know.

Walking around the desert listening to the sand whisper just isn’t good enough.

Strains the ears, not to mention the mind.

Perhaps that’s why so many of you are mad fools.

Not right in the head from trying to listen to the silent sands.

Here, you can hear the sand shouting at you.

When I stumbled into it the first time, all unawares, it simply whispered

“Welcome home”

entries.title: Petruso Conversation 4
entries.text:
The sandstorm is a living thing summoned from the aether by foul sorceries.

Bound here by the same dark rituals.

It escaped the bonds of its masters aeons ago, and knows nothing more than an ageless nameless hunger.

It seems to like me though.

You, not so much.

entries.title: Petruso Conversation 5
entries.text:
I tire of conversing through this madman, Exile.

I desire release from this plane of existence.

Such is not within your power to grant without the Scourgestone.

The Scourgestone was shattered when I was unleashed upon the world.

Each piece was lost to time.

As I sweep across the Exiled Lands, I seek the pieces that would provide my liberation.

And yet, though I touch all, I do not find what I seek.

They are locked away, in caves where the howling sands cannot reach.

Search the Passage, used by the Lemurians to wage war upon the one who unleashed me.

Search the tomb of Gallaman.

He once boasted to his friends as they quivered, caught in my wind,

of the lucky stone that would be buried alongside him.

Search the eastern coastline,

for the pirates whisper to one another of a stone that grants their captain wishes.

Combine these pieces, Exile. Recreate the Scourgestone.

Complete the Archivists ritual.

Only then, can I return whence I came.

entries.title: Princess Conversation 1
entries.text: My blood, it is royal, and apparently coveted for sacrifice. My father is a king...and a dog.

entries.title: Princess Conversation 2
entries.text: Through his own embarrassing indiscretions, my father became indebted to the Stygian Toth-amon. I was traded to settle his accounts and save his craven skin.

entries.title: Princess Conversation 3
entries.text: Oh, these sorcerers. They are all alike. May each and every one of them lose their odious heads!

entries.title: Princess Conversation 4
entries.text: I'm well away from my dog-king father. I won't go back. For now, I'll follow Conan. If that path leads me to danger, so may it be. It can be no worse than what I have faced.

entries.title: Raid Merc Captain Initial Greeting
entries.text:
(gruff grunt of acknowledgement)

(gruff grunt of acknowledgement)

(gruff grunt of acknowledgement)

Ah... It's you.

Serpents and stars... what do you want?

Speak.

Speak.

Ah, greetings.

Ah, greetings.

entries.title: Raid Merc Captain Greeting
entries.text:
(gruff grunt of acknowledgement)

(gruff grunt of acknowledgement)

(gruff grunt of acknowledgement)

Ah... It's you.

Serpents and stars... what do you want?

Speak.

Speak.

Ah, greetings.

Ah, greetings.

entries.title: Raid Merc Captain Conversation 1
entries.text:
Serpents and stars! Freedom and a good blade are the only things I hold sacred. You've won my favor if not my friendship.

What can I do for you in turn? I can sell you a banner under which my mercenaries will unite to defend you and yours.

What's more, I can sell you war supplies -- food, siege equipment, and the like -- all at discount I wouldn't offer my own brother. He's a drunk and a fiend... but that is another story.

I'm as grizzled as they come. I've got a long history, written in blood, helping kings and princes win their little wars.

I'm as grizzled as they come. I've got a long history, written in blood, helping kings and princes win their little wars.

I was once a member of Zarallo's Free Companions. Heard of them? Of course you have!

I was once a member of Zarallo's Free Companions. Heard of them? Of course you have!

But eventually, I struck out on my own. Gathered my closest siblings in slaughter under my own flag. We slinked away in the night. For greater glory! Haven't found it yet... but it's the journey. Ha!

entries.title: Razmaof Shem Initial Greeting
entries.text:
I wanted to thank you…

The woman that you met in those ruins… That was not me.

She was ancient and wicked.

And powerful.

entries.title: Razmaof Shem Greeting
entries.text:
You’ve come back. It is good to see you.

How is your journey, friend?

entries.title: Razmaof Shem Farewell
entries.text:
It has been pleasant. Return when you can, friend.

Be careful, friend. This land is not what it seems.

May Ishtar and Pteor bless your path.

entries.title: Razmaof Shem Conversation 1
entries.text:
You almost killed me in that city. I cannot begrudge you that.

Such a terrible, awesome creation. While I wore the mask, I was somebody else.

I was something else.

Such power and knowledge. I stood on an island in a vast ocean.

I could see across the water in every direction. And that ocean was time.

I… I am sorry. It is difficult to describe.

entries.title: Razmaof Shem Conversation 2
entries.text:
All I have are fragments

Her memories evaporate from my mind like mists above the oasis.

If you have questions, you must ask them quickly.

My mind is leaking, purging itself of all traces of that… woman.

She knew so much. About these bracelets we wear, about the Giant-kings and the ancient war.

About the darkness that lurks in the volcano and…

Just ask, friend, before I lose the nerve to speak.

entries.title: Razmaof Shem Conversation 3
entries.text:
The bracelets? She made them together with the Giant-kings.

The bracelets were imbued with the power of seven artefacts.

The Witch Queen’s Mask. The Diadem of the Giant-kings. The Tears of Two Races.

The Shining Trapezohedron. The Star of the Champion. The Heart of the Sands.

And the Black Ring of Set.

The Bracelets were created to translate any language for the wearer, to allow swift movement around the empire

and to allow communication and recording through the lorestones.

It was only later, when the war began, that the Queen suspected that the Giant-kings had included a deathweave in the sorcery.

Any who tried to flee their control, were simply killed by the curse wall.

entries.title: Razmaof Shem Conversation 4
entries.text:
Her people, the Lemurians, were the remnant of a desperate race.

Her memories before the Exiled Lands are vague and confused.

Flashes of an island kingdom sinking as the world heaves.

A people being subjugated with fire and sorcery.

An escape and a long, treacherous journey into the West.

I’m sorry… it’s fading so quickly.

I remember a daughter, Telith and a great betrayal. And a…

grandchild?

She was a mother and a grandmother.

She knew what it was to be a slave and didn’t want that for any of her people.

She would stop at nothing to prevent it from happening again.

Can you blame her?

entries.title: Razmaof Shem Conversation 5
entries.text:
Her mind shies away from two things – her grandchild and what her people found in the Volcano.

Her grandchild was… not truly human?

Xaltar? He was a hostage? Or something else?

I’m sorry, for her these memories were laden with emotion.

I see a beloved daughter and a hated foe and a child that brings hope.

It is so very unclear.

As for what her people discovered at the volcano?

Horror, anger and disgust.

And fear.

This is the only memory where the Witch Queen of Lemuria felt fear.

entries.title: Razmaof Shem Conversation 6
entries.text:
I truly don’t know what I will do now that I have my freedom.

Conan has offered to accompany me north, back to my home city in Shem.

My palace awaits me and the pampered life of a princess of Shem.

But my enemies, the ones who sent me here.

They also await me there.

Perhaps instead, I will travel with Conan a while.

See the world by his side. He is formidable, yes?

For a barbarian.
