entries.title: Sculptor EL Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Greetings, wanderer. I suspect an interesting and winding road has brought you to me.

I am a sculptor. My current medium is blood crystals. Should you bring me some, I am happy to barter with my skills, such as they are.

entries.title: Sculptor EL Greeting
entries.text:
Greetings. Have you brought me more blood crystals?

Yes-yes, do you have the crystals?

entries.title: Sculptor EL Farewell
entries.text:
Farewell, wanderer.

Farewell, wanderer.

May I soon enjoy your company again... and the blood crystals that you bring me.

entries.title: Sculptor EL Conversation 1
entries.text: So many disparate and desperate roads lead to the perdition of these Exiled Lands.

entries.title: Sculptor EL Conversation 2
entries.text: Once, in another life it seems, I was an artisan of Khitai, my work displayed in the capital cities and honored with no small amount of praise.

entries.title: Sculptor EL Conversation 3
entries.text: Mek-kamoses has shown generosity to we Khitan. He unites us with the promise of escape. His mind is ruthless, but sane... no small feat for one who practices his art.

entries.title: Sculptor EL Conversation 4
entries.text: A powerful statesman commissioned a statue in his image. I toiled for weeks searching for his likeness imprisoned in the block of jade.

entries.title: Sculptor EL Conversation 5
entries.text: I chiseled. I cut. I perspired. Yet every time I neared completion, the jade shattered inexplicably. After the fifth failure, my patron sent me home in a fit of rage. That night, I was abducted. I awoke here.

entries.title: Sculptor EL Conversation 6
entries.text: I have seen many oddities in my time here. I now believe my former patron to be cursed. Only a curse could keep me from freeing him from the jade.

entries.title: Sculptor EL Conversation 7
entries.text: Now, like you, like so many others, I am banished to this uncanny prison. Yet the impossible possibility of escape has manifested.

entries.title: Sculptor EL Conversation 8
entries.text: Mek-kamoses requires my hands to craft an altar to his exact specifications to amplify his sorcery, that he might open a gateway of egress from this cursed place.

entries.title: Sculptor EL Conversation 9
entries.text: Should our gambit succeed, should we escape, I fear we must live in obscurity. I will have to perform my craft anonymously. Even escape is a sort of prison.

entries.title: Sculptor EL Conversation 10
entries.text: And now the prospect of escape fills me with uncertainty. Should I remain here, life would be dangerous. I could be flayed in my sleep. However, the same is true for me in Khitai.

entries.title: Sculptor Siptah Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Greetings, wanderer. I suspect an interesting and winding road has brought you to me.

I am a sculptor. My current medium is blood crystals. Should you bring me some, I am happy to barter with my skills, such as they are.

entries.title: Sculptor Siptah Greeting
entries.text:
Greetings. Have you brought me more blood crystals?

Yes-yes, do you have the crystals?

entries.title: Sculptor Siptah Farewell
entries.text:
Farewell, wanderer.

Farewell, wanderer.

May I soon enjoy your company again... and the blood crystals that you bring me.

entries.title: Sculptor Siptah Conversation 1
entries.text: So we meet again, wanderer. I remain a devoted and humble practitioner of my sculpting craft. Strange we should meet again in this place.

entries.title: Sculptor Siptah Conversation 2
entries.text: I am buffeted by fate, helpless to its whims. My story is... improbable.

entries.title: Sculptor Siptah Conversation 3
entries.text: I know not what to call it... shall we say a mysterious vortex? An aperture that defies all reason swallowed me whole, hurled me upon this Isle of Siptah.

entries.title: Sculptor Siptah Conversation 4
entries.text:
Here, I remain. I would be delighted to make commerce with you, to trade sculpted parts for more blood crystals.

I must keep to my work, keep my hands busy, lest my mind gnaw on all the impossibilities.

entries.title: Shamalla Initial Greeting
entries.text:
You dog! How dare you not grovel before the Queen of The Harpies?!

Those eyes… Resilient, proud, impudent… You are not a common Hyborian who seeks the strong to rule them.

How interesting… Know, Exile, that before you is Shamalla, conqueror of the coast of Zingara and Queen to the pirates of the Exile lands.

You shall speak of what brought you here.

entries.title: Shamalla Greeting
entries.text: Kneel exile. What brings you here?

entries.title: Shamalla Farewell
entries.text: I grow tired, be on your way.

entries.title: Shamalla Conversation 1
entries.text:
I know what bothers your mind: how she, the infamous Queen of The Harpy could end up stranded on this shore?

I guess I will indulge you curiosity: dogs of Argossa ambushed us in the open sea.

On the third day they gave up the chase but the ship was already sinking.

By Ishtar I was ready to go to the depths of the sea along with the ship but fate had another plans for me.

entries.title: Shamalla Conversation 2
entries.text:
Are you of supersticious sort Exile?

My men are still afraid of a sea devil's embrace more than my whip.

Near the islands, deep underwater my crewmen found ruins, of those who returned spoke of large pearls growing inside but are too afraid to come back fearing the devils' servants.

But you are different, aren't you impudent one?

Bring me more pearls from those ruins and I shall reward you handsomely.

The boat is waiting you at the shore.

entries.title: Shamalla Conversation 3
entries.text:
Behold the ship that shall bring us back to the sea!

It's not as gorgeous as my Harpy, yet, with a captain like me it will not fail.

entries.title: Slaver Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Come to look at my goods, eh? I’ve got flesh from across the world.

Not too damaged - and not too defiant.

entries.title: Slaver Greeting
entries.text:
A repeat customer, eh?

I will give you a discount, my friend.

entries.title: Slaver Farewell
entries.text:
Come back anytime. I get new flesh all the time.

You’ll be back. Either before the block, or on it.

Come back anytime. I get new flesh all the time.

entries.title: Slaver Conversation 1
entries.text:
Take a look at my stock. They are the finest flesh in all the Exiled Lands.

Some of these slaves are reserved for… special buyers.

But for you I can offer a set price for a set prize.

All I need is your silver, and you can have one of my slaves.

You do not even need to break them.

I cannot tell you if they have any skills, though.

I sell them - I do not talk to them.

Maybe you get lucky, eh?

entries.title: The Archivist Initial Greeting
entries.text:
Do not be afraid, bonded one.

I am just a shadow, a whisper of what once was.

Before the fall, when my people raised nighted pillars of black stone to the sky, I was called the Archivist.

entries.title: The Archivist Greeting
entries.text:
What have you brought me?

The archives hunger for new knowledge.

You return. Tell me of the things you have seen.

entries.title: The Archivist Farewell
entries.text:
To know is to live, human.

Every new learning is a new beginning. Remember that.

Bring me back knowledge.

entries.title: The Archivist Conversation 1
entries.text:
Have you come to seek a way free of the bonding bracelet, human?

There is only one chance and a very slim one at that.

You must assemble the items of power that were present at its creation.

The Diadem of the Giant-kings, the Mask of the Witch Queen, the Tears of Two Races, the Star of the Champion,

the Heart of the Sands, the Shining Trapezohedron and the Serpent Ring of Set.

I fear I set you an impossible quest, human.

entries.title: The Archivist Conversation 2
entries.text:
The Diadem of the Giant-kings was once worn upon the brow of the Priestking.

He fell in battle with Tyros the Deathbringer, in the battlefields of the north.

More than that, I cannot say.

The Priestking was the most passionate and most volatile among us.

I fear his downfall will not have been a simple matter.

entries.title: The Archivist Conversation 3
entries.text:
The Witch Queen of Lemuria was our great enemy, in the dying days of our race.

Her people came to us as refugees, but even as they took our succour, they plotted our downfall.

She was present at the creation of the bracelets, lending her power to ours.

Her fate is unknown to me,

but she always ruled from her city in the swamp to the east.

entries.title: The Archivist Conversation 4
entries.text:
The Tears of Two races were simple to obtain when we performed the ritual.

We simply took our tears and combined them.

But who is left to weep for both races?

Although…tales persist of Tyros the Deathbringer.

He was born of both races, our blood mingled with theirs.

Perhaps his tears could be enough to fulfil the requirement.

Obtaining them would be quite the feat.

entries.title: The Archivist Conversation 5
entries.text:
The Star of the Champion lies buried beneath the sands of the arena in the east.

I remember well the day that Tyros killed the mighty dragon.

He wept to defeat such a noble creature.

He placed the star upon its brow.

As far as I know, there it remains.

entries.title: The Archivist Conversation 6
entries.text:
The Heart of the Sands, or simply the Scourgestone, was an artefact of power.

The Priestking used it to create a weapon, the sandstorm,

that he swore would be our salvation.

He gave it to the Warmaker to unleash upon our enemies.

As far as I know, it was destroyed in the attempt,

but perhaps the Warmaker has more insight.

entries.title: The Archivist Conversation 7
entries.text:
The Serpent Ring of Set…it was our most powerful artefact.

It was kept in a tomb beneath this city

And then one day…a human came.

He had all the words and signs of power.

He passed through every ward and trap we had put in place.

He took the Serpent Ring of Set and he left.

If you wonder why you wear the bracelet, wonder no more.

It must have been this man, who called himself Thoth-Amon.

Dedicate yourself to recovering the other artefacts.

You will not recover the Serpent Ring of Set as long as you are trapped in this empire.

Perhaps…perhaps it will be enough.

entries.title: The Archivist Conversation 8
entries.text:
When you have the artefacts, you must place them on the receptacle, north of here.

Once you have filled this vessel with the artefacts of power, it will create a keystone.

To remove your bracelet, all that is required is to touch it with the keystone.

And then, mortal, you will be free.

But freedom will mean giving up all that these lands have come to offer you.

So, choose wisely.

entries.title: The Archivist Conversation 9
entries.text:
How did I die?

I was betrayed by your kind.

In a place called the Circle of Swords,

I went in good faith to meet the Witch Queen.

She had promised peace.

I was cut down. My bones were left to rot in the wind.

And yet, my spirit has always belonged to the archives, human.

And so, I am here.

And here I shall remain.

entries.title: Valeria Initial Greeting
entries.text: Well, well...look what the tide washed up.

entries.title: Valeria Greeting
entries.text: You still alive? What news of the isle?

entries.title: Valeria Farewell
entries.text: Sharp blades and strong winds, friend.

entries.title: Valeria Conversation 1
entries.text:
Oh, that gods cursed bastard! That goat-raping son of a whore! I’ll cut off his balls and crush them in front of him!

Oh, that gods cursed bastard! That goat-raping son of a whore! I’ll cut off his balls and crush them in front of him!

Oh, that gods cursed bastard! That goat-raping son of a whore! I’ll cut off his balls and crush them in front of him!

What do you want? I’m busy figuring out how to get my revenge on that pig.

What do you want? I’m busy figuring out how to get my revenge on that pig.

What do you want? I’m busy figuring out how to get my revenge on that pig.

The ship was almost finished...and then those dark-hearted bastards swept in and set it all a flame.

The ship was almost finished...and then those dark-hearted bastards swept in and set it all a flame.

The ship was almost finished...and then those dark-hearted bastards swept in and set it all a flame.

It was the Black Corsairs who burned our ship – and they didn’t even seem to have a reason for it. Could have just as easily been the Stygians.

It was the Black Corsairs who burned our ship – and they didn’t even seem to have a reason for it. Could have just as easily been the Stygians.

entries.title: Vathis Initial Greeting
entries.text:
A pilgrim approaches, yes? The voices in the well told me you'd come.

A pilgrim approaches, yes? The voices in the well told me you'd come.

A pilgrim seeks wisdom, yes?

A pilgrim seeks wisdom, yes?

But in the bedlam of these Exiled Lands, a pilgrim needs a sagacious tongue

But in the bedlam of these Exiled Lands, a pilgrim needs a sagacious tongue

to parse out the wisdom from the chaos.

to parse out the wisdom from the chaos.

And who is more sagacious than I?

And who is more sagacious than I?

I am Vathis, hierophant of the Votaries of Skelos.

I am Vathis, hierophant of the Votaries of Skelos.

entries.title: Vathis Greeting
entries.text:
A pilgrim returns. The voices in the well said as much.

Once more, a pilgrim seeks the wisdom of Vathis, yes?

A pilgrim returns. The voices in the well said as much.

Once more, a pilgrim seeks the wisdom of Vathis, yes?

entries.title: Vathis Farewell
entries.text:
A pilgrim departs.

The voices of the well shall deliver us from this place.

A pilgrim departs.

The voices of the well shall deliver us from this place.

entries.title: Vathis Conversation 1
entries.text:
My past? I was a scholar, vizier to the king of Ophir,

a royal commission and a round belly.

Never mind my past.

Calamity put me on the road that takes all lost things to these Exiled Lands.

I wandered north, past fertile highlands,

past frozen slopes, all the way to the caldera of the volcano.

The time-gnawed ruins piqued my curiosity.

Here I found the well. Here I founded my cult.

And here I remain until I puzzle out the secret to escaping this prison.

entries.title: Vathis Conversation 2
entries.text:
When I realized that the voices in the well were the key to salvation,

I cast off my earthly possessions.

I shed my old life like an old skin.

I recruited followers. The devout became the Votaries of Skelos.

Amid cyclopean ruins we live and study.

Every night, we march in a line, robe-cloaked and torch-bearing.

Every night, we cast our offerings into the well.

We listen...

Only I have glimpsed the Book of Skelos.

Only my tongue knows the forked path.

Only I can translate the hissing whispers in the dark.

The voices in the well enlighten.

Someday soon, I will puzzle out their riddles and escape these Exiled Lands.

entries.title: Vathis Conversation 3
entries.text:
At the volcano's heart, I found a well.

Down it extended, down into darkness infinite.

All through the night, I sat by the well.

I heard sibilant voices from below. Such voices!

They crooned in the dim in tongues

I half-remembered  from my glimpse into the Book of Skelos so many years ago.

But an unprepared pilgrim must never approach the well.

Its nighted cadence would flay your soul.

A pilgrim understands, yes?

Only the worthy of Skelos may approach the well.

But for a courteous pilgrim, I could serve as intermediary.

This is acceptable, yes?

entries.title: Vathis Conversation 4
entries.text:
In my youth, I happened upon the Book of Skelos.

The tome sought after by many and many a sorcerer.

In its pages, I glimpsed the nameless sagas.

I sounded the grim grammar that enslaves the elements,

occasions the dead to rise,

and summons demons from the Outer Dark.

In its pages, I spied the graveyard of the universe --

through the gulfs of space and wells of night.

In its pages, I gazed into the pits of Arallu.

In its verses I saw the Chaos that Crawls.

I enunciated a single syllable of the true name of Tsathoggua!

entries.title: Warmaker Klael Initial Greeting
entries.text: I smell you, human. The stench of your kind has not improved throughout the aeons.

entries.title: Warmaker Klael Greeting
entries.text:
Why do you disturb me?

Leave me alone, human.

I have no time for the chittering of simians.

entries.title: Warmaker Klael Farewell
entries.text:
Everything ends, human.

The ages turn, human.

entries.title: Warmaker Klael Conversation 1
entries.text:
Ashes and dust, human. Ashes and dust.

All that remains of what we once were. An echo of an echo.

In my mind, I still walk the streets of our city. The iron scent of blood as I pass the temple of the old serpent.

The thrumming power in my veins as I chant the ritual of summoning. The intoxication and ecstasy of knowledge.

Ashes and dust.

entries.title: Warmaker Klael Conversation 2
entries.text:
When the world was shattered, your people came to us as refugees

We were generous, we gave them the wild north.

They built a city there amongst the trees, and practiced weird sciences and worshipped their demon gods.

For a thousand years, there was peace and trade among our peoples.

But your kind is ever-seeking, ever-grasping, ever-multiplying.

War was inevitable and when it came, it was terrible.

entries.title: Warmaker Klael Conversation 3
entries.text:
I see that you wear the bracelet, human.

When the war between our peoples began, we created such to bend the prisoners of war to our bidding.

My kind are long lived and slow to replenish our numbers, and as we dwindled in the war, we increasingly came to rely on human prisoners to provide us with labor.

To that end, we created the bracelets, to bond your kind, to control your movements, to allow you to understand our language and to prevent your kind from escaping.

But that was an aeon ago. To what purpose they have been attuned now... I cannot say.

entries.title: Warmaker Klael Conversation 4
entries.text:
I was once of the triumvirate that ruled this land.

The Archivist concerned himself primarily with the collection of knowledge and the Priestking had dominion over our spirits and the rituals of our faith.

My people called me the Warmaker, for it was my duty to keep them safe.

And for a while, I did my duty. I held the northern passes against the humans.

Together with the Archivist, I established the watch towers to warn us of the coming of the humans.

They burn still, human. When their color changes it is because those of your kind have crossed the borders of our kingdom with the intent to slay, burn and kill.

entries.title: Warmaker Klael Conversation 5
entries.text:
In the end, we were betrayed.

During a skirmish in the north, the humans revealed a new weapon forged of their strange science.

It smashed through the ranks of my troops, turning those captured in its blast into solid stone.

Those who survived fled south to lick our wounds.

During this moment of weakness, the Priestking came to me with a proposal.

A new ritual to counter the human sciences, a powerful magic to scour the landscape and drive them from our lands.

In desperation, I agreed.

Do you understand, human?

In ages past, this was the most fertile land in our empire.

And then I unleashed the Sandstorm. I lost control of it.

I betrayed my people to a slow, dwindling doom.

entries.title: Warmaker Klael Conversation 6
entries.text:
I have been alone in the darkness for a long time, human. Few have come to me over the aeons.

But there was a man who came here, years ago.

I had not spoken with another being for thousands of years and there was a hunger in me for conversation.

He did not wear a bracelet, as you do, so he learned my speech.

And he listened to me talk of this city and what it once was. And for a while, as I described it to him, it lived again in my mind.

Perhaps I spoke too much.

When I told him of the greatest artefact of our religion, the Serpent Ring of Set, he left me to search for it.

And now your kind come into my city again, wearing the slave bonds and disturbing the dead.

Time has taught me that coincidences are simply patterns that I have not understood yet...

entries.title: Yakira Initial Greeting
entries.text:
The twin serpents whisper, and a stranger approaches.

Call me priestess. Call me sultana. Call me exalted by the flesh and souls of my people.

I sing the lusty hymns to Derketo.

I submit to the Goddess of Pleasure and the Queen of the Dead so that all others submit to me.

entries.title: Yakira Greeting
entries.text:
You return. Have you come to submit to screaming bliss?

You must return, just as the ocean must swell at the moon's invisible caress.

entries.title: Yakira Farewell
entries.text:
Return when you want me to awaken the coiled splendor within.

May the twin serpents go with you.

entries.title: Yakira Conversation 1
entries.text:
I am the high priestess to the mysteries of Derketo.

I perform the sacred obscenities.

The haughty priests of Mitra and Set frown down upon my wanton rites.

"Holy concubine" they call me, when I am listening,

and "cultish whore" when they think that I am not.

Yet one by one, these clerics offer themselves to me willingly.

I kiss the jelly right out of their skull sockets, and they scream,

and they beg me to do it again, and they see the universe and their gods as they truly are,

and they weep black blood when they realize they have but two eyes to give me.

entries.title: Yakira Conversation 2
entries.text:
Derketo is my goddess.

From her throne of horn and bone, she rules over fertility and the underworld.

In her right hand, she holds a lotus bloom.

In her left hand, she holds the twin serpents Lust and Death.

There is only Lust and Death -- the little death and the endless sleep.

Derketo awakens the goddess in me.

Through her, I have teased the savage animal and enslaved the living clay.

Through her, I have freed the minds of my people to orgiastic frenzy.

Through her, I have made the living beg, the dead moan,

and given some skulls more reason than others to grin.

entries.title: Yakira Conversation 3
entries.text:
My people are the Lemurians.

Ours is an ancient story, cast out of island cities that were ancient before the histories were written.

Time and again, my people met betrayal and enslavement. But always, we break free.

Now we find ourselves in this jungle. Through Derketo, I have given my people transcendent pleasures.

My people have given themselves over to Derketo so completely that they are now free to shed the skin of mere reality.

In these jungles too stalk the ancestors of the Lemurians. They emulated beasts, lay with beasts, and became beasts.

I do not begrudge them their path.

entries.title: Yakira Conversation 4
entries.text:
Do you seek the mysteries of Derketo? Do you dare the transcendent ecstasies?

Would you awaken the sleeping deity swaddled in the cradle of your flesh?

The twin serpents live inside all of us. The Coiled Splendor.

The uninitiated merely spill these energies through their pelvis.

But I can teach you the ways to coax the twin serpents up your spine, up the cosmic ladder, into my mind and hands.

With the twin serpents, Lust and Death, so mastered,

you can perform the miracles of Derketo.

entries.title: A Confession
entries.text:
In the silence of my soul, I know that all of this is my fault.
When my belly began to swell, with Raith’s child, I was happy. He was happy. Even his family, who had always viewed me with suspicion, were happy.
When the menfolk left on a weeklong hunt, the women bathed me in lavender and rubbed scented oils into my belly. They burned beeswax candles and we talked into the night.
When I lay down to sleep, I was full and happy as I had never been before.
I said a prayer to Crom to give my child strength.
I said a prayer to Crom.
Everything that followed – the raiders who came and took us from our homes, the sorcery that prevents us from leaving and even…even the loss of my child. I brought this doom upon us all.

entries.title: A Prayer to Hanuman
entries.text:
Oh lord of the darkness,

Of men who are not men,

Give us strength and suffering,

In equal measure.

Let our bodies be pleasing to you,

And our minds quieted from this conscious thought.

Let us find the inner rages,

And loose them upon the earth.

We give our sacrifice into your hands,

Appease your thirst in the slickness of blood.

Grant us the power of your might,

The eternal strength of Hanuman.

entries.title: Acid-Eaten Journal
entries.text: This book, bound in thick leather, has been almost destroyed by the acid that recently filled the pool. There are bare fragments of pages left, but there is enough to piece together a recipe for a special armor crafted from the skins of reptiles.Among the fragments of the pages you find a single initial, ZK, which is the only clue as to the author’s identity.

entries.title: Adventurer\'s Journal
entries.text:
I do not know what I have found here. This architecture is not familiar to me, yet... it speaks to me. Entices me. It wants me to explore it further. I have heard voices in a foreign tongue coming from the other doors and I have no interest in finding out whom they belong to.

This door interests me though - it is blocked by some sort of mechanism. It may have something to do with these statuettes that I have found. Well, they were lying around the place so I picked them up.

I will try my luck with opening this door.

They don't call me Biorn The Lucky for nothing!

entries.title: Adventurer\'s Note #1
entries.text: The Darfari horde chased us into an old ruin. It was a dead end. I thought it was over. Dear Semat stepped forth bravely to hold the entrance, but the leader of the Darfari, a wild brute with mud and twigs in his hair, cut him down. I was preparing to make my final stand, when the very ground in the chamber began to rumble. The walls shook and the earth moved. The savages fled, wailing, through the passageway, as ground beneath them began to rise. It was a staircase! This was the entrance we had been searching for!Quickly, I stepped over to Semat’s corpse, just long enough to close his eyes and grab his gear. Then I headed down the staircase into darkness. As my eyes adjusted, I came into a large cavern. What a sight! The floor is covered in ankle deep water and ghostfire burns above deeper pools whose murky depths are hard to fathom. Phosphorescent growths cling to the water’s edge and in their dim glow, large creatures are moving in the water. I must be cautious. If the treasure is truly down here, I will need to stay alive in order to find it.

entries.title: Adventurer\'s Note #2
entries.text: At first, I thought that I had come to a dead end and that my quest for the treasure would be over. But then I noticed the stone mechanisms set high into the walls along the corridors. They were built for a taller race than ours (who knows what primordial race once stalked these corridors?), but they can be activated from below with a thrown object or an arrow.There is something else…a voice. It speaks to me and…It is probably nothing. The strain of the long quest in search of my ancestor’s treasure.

entries.title: Adventurer\'s Note #3
entries.text: The path forward grows less clear and yet with every chamber that I conquer, my certainty grows. This is the place where my ancestor, the legendary Zapayo da Kordava came in search of his ultimate dream.The voice in my mind whispers to me of the treasure that awaits me, she praises my strength and conviction. She tells me where to go and how to navigate this maze of confusing chambers.This is the path and soon I will have both the secrets of my ancestor and I will meet this mysterious lady who speaks in my mind.

entries.title: Adventurer\'s Note #4
entries.text: This is it! I can feel her presence just ahead of me! It is time to meet my love, and recover the precious journal of Zapayo da Kordava. If my father is correct, it contains the last recipe that he wrote before he went missing, designed based upon his adventures in the Black Kingdoms to the south.But this is secondary to meeting my love. She awaits me just ahead, and her sweet voice sings in my mind. She is beautiful. I will take her with me back to Zingara and make her my wife. All else is secondary, including the journal of my ancestor.She is telling me to put down my weapons and my possessions…why not? I have nothing to fear from such a gentle spirit…

entries.title: Age-worn Scroll
entries.text:
3 days after The Fall 

The world has stopped shaking. The dust has cleared. We live. 

But the world we knew is gone. Our city has been swallowed by the earth. Many have died, crushed beneath the rubble. More are wounded and trapped. We hear their screams echoing in the gloom, but we cannot find them.

Why has Set abandoned us?

entries.title: Age-worn Scroll
entries.text:
7 days after The Fall

Sanity slowly returns. Those who survived have assembled in the temple district to take stock. All told, over a thousand of us still live. Men, women and children.

The event has come to be known as The Fall, which is an apt description for the moment when the earth swallowed our city. We still do not know what we have done to anger the Great Serpent, but the priests are burning sacrifices day and night.

Yes, we still live, but there is no light aside from the fire we create ourselves and several species of luminescent fungi. Our survival depends on working together.

Khor, who has a reputation as a mystic and a sage, has suggested that we turn to the library for our salvation - poring over the histories that have survived in search of clues to help with our situation.

I know that this is something that I can help with.

entries.title: Age-worn Scroll
entries.text:
33 days after The Fall

As things grow more desperate without, I have found a ray of hope in the writings of the past. Though our candles have long since been burned away, I have taught myself to read in the dim luminescent light of the fungi - and in that eerie glow I found a ritual and a name. Thag.

The prostrations of the priests have earned us nothing. If our god has turned against us, perhaps it is time for us to summon a new god.

entries.title: Age-worn Scroll
entries.text:
48 days after The Fall

Khor and his advisors are considering the ritual as an alternative. Scouts have been exploring the cavern but their explorations have borne no fruit. We are trapped and our food supplies dwindle. Rumor has it that there are those in the commons who have taken to eating human flesh.

Set has truly abandoned us.

entries.title: Age-worn Scroll
entries.text:
57 days after The Fall

Our situation grows ever more desperate. The food is gone. There are no animals left in the city and cannibalism is rampant. Even the rocks have been scraped bare of their lichen.

Khor has presented the idea of the ritual to the people - and it did not go well. The people are scared and scared people turn to the gods for help.

The priests were only too willing to denounce our apostacy and they have locked the doors of the temple against us. Khor is determined however, and he is not likely to be thwarted.

entries.title: Age-worn Scroll
entries.text:
93 days after The Fall

The ritual will proceed. The only course that remains for us is apostasy. To renounce the god who has abandoned us and seek new allies in the darkness.

Khor is confident that he can control whatever the ritual summons. He has demonstrated his powers in the past. The doors of the temple have been shattered and the priests confined to their living quarters.

We are prepared.

entries.title: Age-worn Scroll
entries.text:
94 days after The Fall

The ritual has gone wrong. The demon known as Thag has escaped confinement. The screams of the priests echo across the city and the temple has become a slaughterhouse.

We are fools. We have locked ourselves in the darkness with a fiend. I want to feel pity for the unsuspecting denizens of the other quarters, but I can only feel a bone-deep fear that settles on my soul.

We plan to get out, to find a space outside the limits of the city. Perhaps the demon will tire of the slaughter. Perhaps we will find safety. Perhaps we can find hope.

entries.title: Age-worn Scroll
entries.text:
97 days after The Fall

It has hounded us from the city. We are all that remains.

Khor believes that he can capture the creature, by reversing the ritual. He needs to return to the temple, through the devastated streets of the city. I wish him well, but I will take my chances here, on the fringes. About half of our party will go with him, while the rest remain here with me.

We will pray to Set for his success.

entries.title: Age-worn Scroll
entries.text:
107 days after The Fall

Khor has failed.

We thought we knew what darkness was but the shadows we knew were nothing more than the absence of light. This darkness is the end of hope.

Thag is coming and we cannot stand against it. This is the end.

entries.title: Archery Note
entries.text: Little brother, Got yourself so deep into something that even The Chief couldn\'t pull your arse off the fire.  Everyone\'s nervous about the move. Father\'s being a nightmare, sitting by your bed and weeping, then going around bellowing at people.  You\'ve really made a mess of things.  I wanted to stay, so did Freya and a bunch of the others, but father is insisting.  You\'re the better brawler, but I\'ve always been the better hunter. I don\'t know what this doom is that the Elders are babbling on about, but I\'ve never seen anything that kept moving after it took an arrow through the eye.  So I left you my best bow. Just draw and shoot, you\'ll be fine.  I hope you\'ll be fine, little brother.  And if you survive you can never tell anybody that I called you the better brawler. Or you\'ll wish the doom had gotten you.  <LoreBold>Drag arrows over a bow in the inventory to equip them to that bow. Shoot the archery targets to get a feel for archery.</>

entries.title: Blood-Stained Note
entries.text:
When I first met Nahjef, I thought his fervor would lead to bountiful spoils. Where other Hunters would squander their coin in the dim-lit dens of debauchery, Nahjef would wisely hoard his earnings; I found his attitude to be refreshing.

For some time, it was a successful partnership; we would plot and plunder, reaping the fruits of others' labors.

I have lost count of the days since that fruit spoiled.

After we seized a hefty prize from a village in the southern sands, Nahjef became secretive. He no longer shared the nature of our next quarry - only that he knew its whereabouts, and all I needed was to follow him. Had he ever led me astray?

But enough is enough. I have been yanked by the collar across this cursed desert, and my patience is spent. Devil take whatever this treasure is he seeks, wherever it lies! I shall bid him luck after tomorrow's hunt and strike out on my own.

There's something strange in his eyes: a dogged drive that I cannot fathom. He plans to drag me into that dank and musty sewer, where I am certain no treasure awaits - yet again.

I fear he is chasing phantoms.

entries.title: Bonepile
entries.text:
Here lies Thick

Stepped on a tick

Unfortunate was he

That the tick giant be

entries.title: Bonepile
entries.text: Here lies Appsro\n\nIn life he bragged he would be able to come back to haunt us from six feet under. So we buried him at eight.

entries.title: Bonepile
entries.text: Neebs\n\nStabbed, flayed, burnt, gutted, torn apart, beheaded, deboned, poisoned, stung by bees, chopped up, hacked, and hung like a horse.

entries.title: Bonepile
entries.text:
Doraleous

Seeker of the holy relic
Protector of the downtrodden
Wielder of the Zephyr Blade
Sort of

entries.title: Bonepile
entries.text:
Here lies Simon

Found himself in battle with a giant spider and was unable to dodge right.
Now there's nothing left.

entries.title: Bonepile
entries.text: Adahop\n\nAfraid of many things but it turns out giant demonic spiders was not amongst them. That was very unfortunate.

entries.title: Cannoneer\'s Woes
entries.text:
Fire!
Fire!
Fire!
All I hear is the captain's voice in my head screaming that word.
I hate it. All I want is a plot of land to myself where I can till the ground. Grow my tulips.
I hope I never hear the word ever again.

entries.title: Cargo list
entries.text:
Today’s delivery from Sepermeru arrived:
Wooden planks – 2’000
Iron reinforcements – 1’500
New recruits – 10
Steel bars – 1’000
All accounted for.
Queen will be pleased; at this rate we will get this ship sailing before the storm season and leave this cursed land once and for all.

entries.title: Carpentry Note
entries.text: Sennu and his band of fools broke the gates on their last raid and whether you believe in this doom or not, a village without gates is a vulnerable place.  We have most of what is needed stored around here. Just remember what you learned when we made the western gates last summer. Crafter Sebalia  <LoreBold>Open your inventory and find the recipe for gates in the crafting panel on the right. Use the crafting stations to craft the ingredients you need for the gates. </>

entries.title: Collector\'s Note
entries.text:
Bel and Ishtar, my life has taken a strange turn since the shipwreck.

My children my menagerie, all drowned in their cages on the open ocean along with the crew. I washed ashore, alone and despairing. For some while, I concerned myself with the basics of survival.

But as food and water became less of a concern, I noticed the wildlife. And what variety, what beauty!

There are lizards the like of which I have never seen! Birds with plumage that strikes the breath from my lungs! Predators and prey living together on this lonely isle, creatures unheard of in the known lands.

I began slowly, first attracting the attention of the feral dogs which roam the wilderness in packs. I suspect they are not native to the island - probably introduced by whoever built the old ruins that dot the island. I earned their trust with meat and a firm hand - now I rule this small pack.

I have trained them to hunt - to chase other animals to exhaustion and to lead them to me.

My path is clear, I will rebuild my menagerie from the exotic creatures of this island. I will flag down a ship and barter for passage back to the mainland. And from there ... the courts of kings and untold riches await!

I will lead my small pack inland today, and we shall begin our collection.

entries.title: Costigan\'s Note #1
entries.text:
Honest to cats, I never been so confused in me life. We shipped out from the Port o Manila and right into a howler of a storm. I was restin' in me bunk aboard the Sea Girl on account of a knuckle-duster I got into with Bat Slade of the Dauntless. 

Next minute I was drinkin' sea water and swimmin' for me life. Wound up on a beach with the pieces of this wreck. 

I hope it ain't the Sea Girl in bits here on the sand. Mushy Hansen and One-Round Grannigan and Flat-Face O'Toole and Swede Hjonning and the rest o them mankillers gone to Davy Jones. Not the Old Man – still ain't forgiven him for kickin' me offa the Sea Girl that incident with the pole-cat. 

Mike, me white bulldog, is swaggerin' around here though. I gotta find the American Bar and get some directions.

entries.title: Costigan\'s Note #2
entries.text:
Now I am the soul of politeness and never pull no rough stuff without provocation but the natives on this island are a rough bunch. 

Ugliest fellow I seen, with the crookedest face I ever seen on a human being. Lurched at me outta the woods and hit me with a hard left. I went down fast, but good ole Mike latched onto his ankle while I got up. Now I been champeen o the Sea Girl for too long to let some ugly-man from the islands give me a beating. 

A left and right under the heart buckled his knees, and a blazing right to the temple glazed his eyes. He reeled, and a trip-hammer left hook to the jaw that packed all my beef sent him crashing to the ground. 

He was flattened alright, and I told Mike to leave off chewing at his leg. 

Still lookin' for a bar.

entries.title: Costigan\'s Note #3
entries.text: By Jerusha, I\'m getting sick o this place. Mike and I swaggered across the island and we found this outdoor waterhole.

entries.title: Crafting Note
entries.text: Your father asked that we leave you a message. He never said it needed to be polite.  So take a look around. This is the livelihood of the village. The forge, the tannery, the carpenters…all of it to be left behind because of you, you thrill-seeking fool!  We\'ll have to start from scratch, someplace else. Because you had to explore ruins best left to rot in peace!  Our one saving grace is that we know how to harvest starmetal. With that secret we can make a comfortable life almost anywhere.  So here it is, your father wanted you to forge your own sword of starmetal before… well you know what is coming.  Everything you need, you\'ll find in this room. Except enough starmetal – so read this next part carefully.  The secret is fire. You need to crack the outer shell of the star metal with a fire orb. It will crumble and give you access to glowing core. Harvest that with a pick.   Go west to where the stars fall and gather enough starmetal to craft yourself a fine sword. It\'ll give your father something nice to bury you with.  Crafter Sebalia  <LoreBold>Remember to take fire orbs and a pick when you go to harvest starmetal. </>

entries.title: Crossing the Wall
entries.text:
Raiders, blood, and fire. That is the last thing that I remember. 
When I awoke, I was in a strange, warm land. I gave thanks to Ymir that I had not yet come to Valhalla, and yet this place of merciless heat and monsters felt unreal to me. On my wrist was a bracelet, wrought with ornate shapes and set with a glowing green gem. In the north, I could see the shapes of mountains and it filled me with a longing for the snows of my homeland.
I set out across the desert.
Along the way I encountered men who were little more than beasts. They spoke fearfully of a curse that wraps this land. Strange pillars that strike down any who try to cross the wall of curses.
I persevered until I reach the northernmost reaches of the desert. And there my path was blocked by the strange pillars that had been described to me. Beyond, I could see fertile lands and the great slopes of the mountains that spoke to my very soul. I dared not cross the borderline, lest I perish.
I sat at the base of the pillar and gave in to exhaustion and despair.
When I awoke, something had changed. The jewel in the bracelet on my arm flamed a pulsing crimson and the wall which blocked my progress flickered and gave way.
Holding my breath, I stepped past the wall.
I have settled myself among my people in the village named New Asagarth. There are Vanir and Aesier mingled here, but the old blood feuds are forgotten and forgiven.
To the north, another walls of curses blocks access to the other side of the mountains. But I am confident that one day, we can escape.
We know that these walls can be breached.

entries.title: Crumbled note
entries.text:
Berries and wine in all glory but when you need the strength of a bear and thunder in your heart, nothing beats a good booze-up!

Within these pages, I have written down the recipes that I know for some of the most foul-smelling, bad-tasting, eye-watering home-brew booze I have ever had the misfortune of tasting.

These cimmerians seem to enjoy them, though. Go figure. A sour drink for a sour people.

entries.title: Crumbled note
entries.text:
How I miss my tavern in Pelishti. I was appreciated there. At the very least until the arrival of mad king Akhirom who banned all wine and threw me to the street. Madness. 

Nevertheless. I am here now and I must make the best of it. My only wish is that I had not run into this pack of wild men that call themselves the Dogs of the Desert. In this forsaken place, the sword and fang rule supreme and there is little I can offer but my expertise in brewing fine wines.

Having said that, these beasts are not entirely without culture and as it happens, I do remember some of the recipes from my tutelage. So far, it has kept them from devouring me. 

For how long this state of affairs will last, I cannot tell.

entries.title: Diary Fragment
entries.text:
Master Strabo, 

I trust this message finds you well, although with so many Zingarans involved, it’s Mitra’s will alone that it finds you at all. 

As our dear Captain Zarano explains it, the messenger gulls are a special breed trained to fly a circuitous path back to their roost in the Barachan Isles. From there, a Zingaran Cog piloted by Zarano’s own second cousin conveys the missive back to our man in Kordava. 

Which brings us to my report -- and that is of slow progress. Although Zarano knows not of my skill as a navigator, I’m certain our current heading takes us farther and wider of the Stygian Coast than estimated. 

And while any mariner would do well to avoid those grim shores, I sense a growing unease within the crew and believe Zarano avoids something else altogether. Perhaps I can steal a glance at the charts and discover what that might be. 

Your servant, 
Milo

entries.title: Diary Fragment
entries.text:
Master Strabo, 

Captain Zarano is a cagey old buzzard (like the gulls he keeps) and offers little access to his chart table. He shares his chamber with Gonzago, the young navigator, and none other are allowed entry. 

Fortunately, the crew like their dice and drink, a fine combination to get anxious tongues wagging. Before part of a flagon was finished, I started hearing tales of ‘the island’. From flesh-eating savages to other unspeakable horrors, not one of the crew could agree on exactly who or what awaited us there. Only that it was a place to be avoided at all cost. 

Mitra knows these aren’t my waters, Master, but any sailor worth his salt has heard more than a handful of tales about haunted ships, shores, and islands, but that’s all they are – tales. 

I must confront Captain Zarano while there is still hope of making our promised delivery. 

Your servant, 

Milo

entries.title: Diary Fragment
entries.text:
Master Strabo, 

The chances at making our promised delivery are fleeting. 

After confronting Captain Zarano over his dreaded ‘ghost island’ and the days lost trying to avoid it, he has retreated into his quarters and taken to drink. 

We remain a week behind plan, but any chance to improve our position continues to be vexed by uncanny weather. Ether we’re forced to lash down as unseasonable gales threaten to wash us away or we’re left adrift on becalmed seas, sweltering under the Stygian sun with nary a gull’s fart to move our sails. 

As an experienced mariner, I’ve never seen a run of ill fortune to match this. Perhaps a fatted calf could be slaughtered in our name at Mitra’s hall. 

The sighting of land has been called from above, I will seal this message and investigate.

 Your servant, 

Milo

entries.title: Diary Fragment
entries.text:
Master Strabo, 

Circumstances continue to prevent our exodus from this accursed stretch of sea. 

We sighted Zarano’s island again last night, damn the navigator’s eyes. And while our pickled Captain swears by his man as a reader of charts, my experience suggests otherwise.

 I told the navigator – nay I ordered the navigator to set course away from the island by the most direct route. And yet, as we awoke with the dawning, there sat the island once again. As if it were waiting for us and would not be denied. 

Zarano maintains it’s a ‘trick of the currents’, but his blackening mood betrays his belief in that fiction. Perhaps the strange flows of these cursed waters play a part, but more goes on here. 

One of the men has reported seeing a light emanating from the island and the crew draws lots to form a shore party. Perhaps I’ll see about this light myself. 

Your servant, 

Milo

entries.title: Diary Fragment
entries.text:
Master Strabo, 

This will be my last message. We have been betrayed. 

Gonzago, the navigator, has led a mutiny against the hapless Captain Zarano. We are locked within the old drunk’s quarters for now, but the mutineers have made clear their intent to put us ashore. 

The crew blames Zarano for our predicament and sends us to the island with more than half of the ship’s riches and cargo. 

‘Tribute’ they call it. Tribute that should have been paid well in advance of our journey they say. No one passes the Isle of Siptah without it. 

Your servant, 

Milo

entries.title: Dispatches from the Black Ring
entries.text:
Praise be, to Father Set and the Ivory Throne. All goes according plan. 

 I gained the shore of Lord Siptah’s domain and provided the customary signal but thus far no response has been returned. 

I hesitate to trespass beyond the margins of the shoreline for the Master’s habits are well-known. Doubtless the jungle teems with all manner of abominations curated from the many hells he visits. 

However, if no response is proffered by morning light, I will be forced to brave the interior in search of Siptah’s vaunted citadel – Father Set’s will be done! 

I only pray he recalls our previous meeting at conclave in Khemi so many years ago – certainly the Master’s impression was not lost upon me. 

While many claim direct lineage to the Khari, I believe Lord Siptah embodies the true spirit of the first folk and remains a loyal subject. 

Which makes his disappearance and lack of tribute all the more vexing. 

Justicar Hakketh

entries.title: Dragonmouth
entries.text:
Stop, Bonded One. Proceed no further.

Beyond this passage lies death and despair.

It is forbidden.

Do not go, or the protections that we have put in place for your kind will activate.

There will be death.

entries.text:
Empty

Empty

Empty

entries.title: Entry from the Book of Skelos
entries.text:
This page appears to have been translated from the book of Skelos. 

Into the west, unknown of man, 
Ships have sailed since the world began. 
Read, if you dare, what Skelos wrote, 
With dead hands fumbling his silken coat; 
And follow the ships through the wind-blown wrack- 
Follow the ships that come not back.
entries.groupLabel: Entry from the Book of Skelos

entries.title: Entry from the Book of Skelos
entries.text:
This page appears to have been translated from the book of Skelos. 

Over the foaming, island reefs, 
Past sunken hulls and bones beneath. 
Seek, if you dare, the emerald isles, 
With gems and gold in heaping piles; 
And follow the path to the sparkling heart - 
Follow it down to the endless dark.
entries.groupLabel: Entry from the Book of Skelos

entries.title: Entry from the Book of Skelos
entries.text:
This page appears to have been translated from the book of Skelos. 

Into the sward, beyond the beach, 
Ignore the fruit that beckons sweet. 
Seek, if you dare, the castle wall, 
Where statues wait in frozen thrall; 
And follow the courtyard to the pool - 
Follow the path to the island's jewel.
entries.groupLabel: Entry from the Book of Skelos

entries.title: Entry from the Book of Skelos
entries.text:
This page appears to have been translated from the book of Skelos. 

Beneath the pool, such hidden depths,
Plunge in without a last regret. 
Seek, if you dare, the god within, 
Calling you from the depths therein; 
And follow your heart to jade black- 
Follow the men that came not back.
entries.groupLabel: Entry from the Book of Skelos

entries.title: Etched Note
entries.text:
By the gods, they’re going to eat me!

The drums are beating in frenzied rhythm and the Darfari savages are dancing around a pit of swirling flames.

I’ve seen what happens to those who are cast into the pit –

the screams as the searing heat consumes them.

If you are, like me, a captive of these devils then I can offer no comfort.

But if you are a free man and can read these words then I would ask a boon of you:

Salaceo of the Black Hand pretended to be my friend and then traded me to these savages for animal hides.

He needed my skills as a scribe to write down directions to the place where he keeps his treasures,

lest he forget the path.

But then because of my knowledge of his hiding place, he sold me to the Darfari.

But I prepared for this eventuality.

I wrote his instructions down incorrectly.

If you find him, kill him. Do not offer quarter or mercy.

Take his instructions, by way of thanks.

But remember that for every step I wrote, I took two.

entries.title: Faded note
entries.text: This is where I die. The bridge collapsed shortly after I arrived here and there is no way down except for the short way.\r\n\r\nHowever - it is better than starving to death. It is the height of irony that I came here foraging for bird eggs to stave off starvation.

entries.title: First Mate\'s Report
entries.text:
As ordered, we tracked the deserters into the southern desert.

We found Old Colby here hidden in the shelter of a rocky outcrop, muttering to himself.

Of the others, there was no sign.

I asked Colby to tell me what happened, and I got this wild tale which I have written down in full.

"We weren’t desertin’, we was off huntin’ for treasure!

Jacoby had it in ‘is mind that them broodin’ ruins, the ones that the bracelet calls The Observatory was full of treasures.

Jacoby got me and the boys to follow along, an’ he took us to the cliff with a length o’ stout rope an’ a plan to go climbin’.

Well, the ascent was steep an’ dangerous, on account of us doin’ it during the nighttime to avoid them cannibals as haunts the area,

but we picked a nice clear night with a full moon and got ourselves to the very top.

I gotta say, I didna like them ruins from the moment we got there.

Just a pile o black brooding stone in the moonlight and no treasure in sight.

Jacoby starts poking around, lookin for the treasure an all and he uncovers one of them glowing tablets.

That voice – you know the one – starts talkin’ about constellations and star signs and the crew starts swearin’ and cursin Jacoby for dragging us up for nothin and then it came…

I ain’t ever seen anything like it, mate.

It had skin like a plague corpse and black bristlin’ fur.

It’s red eyes were glowin’ like the coals of hell and when it reared up, the wings blotted out the moonlight.

It took Jacoby first, just snappin forward and tearing ‘is head clean off.

Me and the boys were screamin’ and scramblin’, trying to reach the rope and get down afore this beasty could take us.

I got to the rope and started slidin’ down, burnin’ my hands as I went.

Above, I could hear the screamin’ and snapping and warm blood was a rainin’ from the sky.

The rope was swayin’ and slappin’ me into the cliffface and I was scramblin’ and then… it let go.

I fell and I fell, but I crashed into the top of a tree and I passed out.

When I awoke, I was alone out here in the desert, with a sandstorm sweepin’ in.

I got meself to some shelter and then you found me here.”

I’m bringing Colby back to the ship for judgement. Personally, I think we can hang him from the edge.

He’s obviously lying to protect the others.

entries.title: Foragers note
entries.text: I have been attempting to scavenge for more mushrooms in the area but have found none. I brought my notes so that I could show my companions the pictures of which mushrooms to forage.\r\n\r\nLast night, Hragnar brought a red-speckled one that I have not seen before. I have made some notes on potential uses for it but I will not be the first to try them out.

entries.title: Gord\'s Journal
entries.text: ... More crates washed ashore today.\nSilk, cracked pottery, jewels – the usual booty the sea provides us with. But this time there were boxes with rare spices to the likes of which I have never seen. Its zesty texture burns the throat but warms the body and brings out the sweat. I’d better start cooking, Shamalla will have me hanged for making her wait...

entries.title: Grave Cairn
entries.text: Here lyes Sanus from Ophir. A coward, a joker and a man with a strange obsession for sheep, but he saved my life. I could not save his. - Braga of Cimmeria

entries.title: Harlot\'s Journal #1
entries.text: The old chieftain, wretch that he is, lies dying now, and I can do little to help as I have no knowledge of the arcane arts or of medicine. My expertise in bedroom acrobatics will be of little use to the old fool - it might even bring him closer to the brink, if not push him over it completely. When he dies, I wonder what will become of me. Still - Chamiel, his son, is strong and not very intelligent. If I play my cards right, I may still have a safe haven here. I think I shall have to pay him a night-time visit to ease his weary mind and muscle. The thought of getting caught is enticing in itself, although the prospect of the wheel is less so. I shall have to be careful.

entries.title: Harlot\'s Journal #2
entries.text: Miserable bastard. The chieftain is infuriatingly strong-willed! He simply refuses to die. To make matters worse, I believe Chamiel has been bragging about our acrobatics to the camp guards, despite my pleas to keep our meetings a secret. The situation could be dangerous, especially if the chieftain recovers and hears the rumors. I may have to leave this place prematurely... perhaps I should head south, past the walls and towards the riverbed.

entries.title: Harlot\'s Journal #3
entries.text: It pays to have an ample supply of lubricants and oils handy. I managed to slip out of the shackles binding me to the wheel of pain during the changing of the guard. I ran through the desert until I could no longer see the village. Skirting the outer walls of the cursed city, I made my way to this place. For a wasteland this desert teems with life and plenty of souls in need of a warm embrace and soft words whispered into their ears. I won\'t settle here, however. The cursed city looms over me like a beast and I cannot relax in its shadow. I also have no intention of waking up to a scorpion skittering between my legs. I have enough of that horseplay from the locals. I will follow the river, and see where it goes.

entries.title: Harlot\'s Journal #4
entries.text: Had I known of the crocodiles, I would have taken my chances in the desert! The scaled monstrosities followed me at a distance and I thought I had lost them. Alas! I was not as lucky as I had hoped. One of the larger ones lunged at me from the water and tore my dancing silks, forcing me to abandon them for plainer fare. Thankfully, the crocodile seemed more interested in thrashing about with my torn clothing, than with me, so I was able to escape. I am exhausted and will make camp here before I move further north. I have seen a spire stretching towards the sky. I will make my way there.

entries.title: Harlot\'s Journal #5
entries.text: I have set up a temporary camp in the area. The tent threatens to blow away in the wind and the ruins whisper to me at night, so I dare not stay here for long. My arms and legs are sore and for all the wrong reasons - travelling in this fashion is a strain on my constitution much worse than any man or woman I ever bedded in the bordellos of Zamoria. It may be wishful thinking but I believe I can hear occasional sounds from the north. There may be an encampment there. Tomorrow I will find out. I miss my bed in the village. I even miss the old chieftain.

entries.title: Harlot\'s Journal #7
entries.text: The encampment has been overrun by raiders and miscreants. I have yet again been forced out from my home. I made my way here, where I knew they would not follow and now I understand why. I hear the breath of the beast, even above the thundering sound of my own heartbeat. I will stay here no longer than I need to in order to make certain I have not been followed. Thankfully I managed to steal enough bread and water to sustain me for a short period. Yesterday night, I saw lights in the south. I will travel there. Perhaps the light from the fires will keep the hyenas at bay.

entries.title: Harlot\'s Journal #8
entries.text: I am wary of these people. I am unfamiliar with their customs and ill at ease in this place despite the warm welcome. My travels have taught me the importance of being careful. I had a dream last night after bedding the high priest. A dream of blood and water. An outstretched hand holding me in the skies only to then lay me down in a river. The eyes of the men here undress me hungrily... which is odd because their dark-skinned women go bare skinned all around.Perhaps it is as my first madame taught me - it\'s what they don\'t see, what they imagine, that fuels their lust.

entries.title: Harlot\'s Journal #9
entries.text: Cannibal savages. I have made my way here, as it seems they will not come close to this area. But the wretched bastards have beaten me still. Something courses through my veins, not the fiery visions of the lotus or the blissful dreams of the poppy, but a dark swirling maelstrom of pain that hovers beneath a tentacled shadow. I should never have drunk their infernal concoction. It is becoming difficult to move. Zath take them! I see now what the dream foretold. A premonition. I go to the embrace of Zath willingly. Perhaps in his afterlife, there are beds of finest spider silk...

entries.title: Hastily scribbled note
entries.text: I fear for my life. These Darfari cannibals are unthinking brutes with no discipline and I do not know how much longer I can convince them to leave me alive.\r\n\r\nThe cooking pots reek of human flesh and my trusty book of recipes counts for naught amongst these savages.

entries.title: Hastily scribbled note
entries.text: A curse on them all. I might\'ve drawn the shortest stick but I will be damned if I scout those blackened walls. If you are reading this, heed my warning and stay away from there. All roads lead to the city, I once heard a man say. Not the road I intend to follow, that much is certain. I will travel along the northern coast instead. Perhaps there is a way around and away from those walls.

entries.title: Horn Note
entries.text: If you are ready to face the doom then blow this horn. When it comes, it will come from the north, along the pass. Whatever preparations you have made, I pray they are enough.  Be steadfast. Our ancestors will give you strength. Use it.  <LoreBold> Blow the horn to summon the Doom of Skyholme and begin the final battle. You will not be allowed to blow it until you have made basic preparations.</>

entries.title: Impaled Skeleton
entries.text: The dry desert winds have mummified this poor wretch. The arrows embedded in his leathery skin cannot be removed without breaking them.

entries.title: Innkeeper\'s Ledger
entries.text: Szeth the Drunk picked another fight today. This time the damage wasn\'t too bad, some smashed up furniture and a couple of barrels busted. Still it might be weeks until someone brings fresh stock from New Asagard. I\'d say he\'s not worth the trouble but the truth is... he drinks about ten times as much as everyone else put together. I\'d probably be out of business without him.

entries.title: Innkeeper\'s Ledger
entries.text: Another fight. It\'s really starting to reach the point where I might need to tell Szeth he can\'t drink here anymore. The other patrons see him coming and most of them leave immediately. Every choice is bad for business.

entries.title: Innkeeper\'s Ledger
entries.text: Hah! Finally fortune smiles on me again. A brute came to the tavern today, naming himself Conan of Cimmeria. Szeth didn\'t like the look of him, of course, and so he picked a fight. Conan beat him bloody! But the barbarian isn\'t a man to hold grudges, he spent the rest of night buying Szeth drinks. He even helped me carry Szeth down into the Wine Cellar after Szeth passed out. I like this Cimmerian.

entries.title: Innkeeper\'s Ledger
entries.text: By the gods, what was I thinking? I locked Szeth the Drunk in the WINE CELLAR! That\'s where all the drinks are! I\'m ruined!

entries.title: Journal
entries.text:
I never thought I would see this much silver in my entire life. This place is a goldmine! Well. Obviously it's not. It's a silvermine. Who cares? It's not like anyone is going to read this journal - I don't even know why I write in it.
 
Thrann has gone back to Sepermeru to find us more explosives to dig deeper as he believes the veins stretches further down into the mountains. If we dig much further, we'll end up in the wine cellar... The less said about THAT place the better, really. Thrann already blocked the way to it by collapsing the tunnel there. Whatever darkness lurks in that place is not something I care to think about.

Instead, we are making progress towards the west, following a promising vein downwards.

entries.title: Journal
entries.text: I may be a complete failure as a navigator but I make an excellent thief! Valeria won\'t find me here. Unfortunately, nor will anyone else. I tore down the entrance while drunk on Valeria\'s personal stash. I have no idea if I can make it back out or not. At least I have these lovely drinks to comfort me as the air runs out. I\'m so lucky.

entries.title: Journal
entries.text: So far so good. We sent Monica to scout for a better location. There is no respite from the wild dogs on this isle. Sooner or later they will break our defenses. I hear screams in the distance now and then. Survivors? I don\'t know. Those screams haunt me at night. I hope that these... murderbirds... is the worst this island has to offer. But those screams... Monica best come back soon.

entries.title: Journal
entries.text: Can\'t be done, my arse. I\'ll show them if it so kills me!

entries.title: Journal
entries.text:
The Journal 1

The Journal 2

The Journal 3

entries.title: Journal Note
entries.text: Fools, all of them. I will show them that my underwater breathing concoction works just as well as that of the Stygian alchemists! Then we shall see who has the last laugh!

entries.title: Journal of a Soldier
entries.text:
I saw him today, the leader of those pirates. Nkosi. 'The Ashen' they call him. The men of the Black Kingdoms have skin of ebony, but he is pale-skinned and red-eyed. 

They say he is a demon who files his teeth to points and consumes the flesh of his enemies in battle. 

Kraxus told us that this island would be ripe for the taking. An easy job with plenty of treasure to bring home. Now we're trapped on the island between those bloody Accursed and the Black Corsairs. 

He really is a bastard.

entries.title: Journal page
entries.text: The giants will not reach us here. All we need to do is to wait them out and leave when we can. I\'m certain it will not take long. I wonder what this smell is, though. It really reeks down here for some reason.

entries.title: Journal page
entries.text: Henry and Oyvind came back from the forest. It should be safe enough there, they say. I have a bad feeling about this - and whatever that godforsaken storm is, it\'s coming closer. I\'ve seen... things. In it. In the storm. We will weather this storm and then push into the forest. Mitra be with us.

entries.title: Last Words
entries.text: Crap.

entries.title: Letter
entries.text:
My dearest,

I lament the dreary circumstances that conspire to keep us separated on these islands. I regret that I sent you south - I hunger for your touch, among other things. N'kosi and his men keep me busy with their little incursions - not to mention the beastly creatures at the heart of the isle. 

I couldn't help but notice that your last missive felt a little…cold. I've told you before that my marriage is one of convenience, and that once we have conquered the island, I will put her aside. 

Naturally since I am of royal blood (albeit the wrong kind), we can never be formally wed. Instead, I will keep a harem and you will be first among many. Yes, I like that idea. 

Cheers, 

Kraxus

entries.title: Letter
entries.text:
Commander Kraxus, 

We have completed our scouting of the Isle - our men have taken to calling it the Isle of Dawn due to the odd color of the rocks here. We have established several major camps in the area - we're repairing the docks of a former Stygian city - probably from the Siptah expedition a generation ago. 

We have established a hunting camp in the center of the isle - game is plentiful here and as long as we hold this land I cannot foresee us running into supply problems. A carefully managed hunting reserve here will keep our troops fed for a long time. 

There is a hostile force on the south of the isle - giant grey-skinned humanoids. They appear to be the natural inhabitants of this isle and they do not speak a civilized tongue. They have captured several of our scouts and dragged them off to their citadels - but I have chosen not to engage them. 

I've sent several men on expeditions to the other isle - the Isle of Dusk. Almost none of my scouts have returned from that gloomy place. I'm weighing my options before sending more men. 

We cannot afford to fight a war on two fronts - my men are focused on the channel and protecting our southern flank from the Black Corsairs. 

Cordially, 

Netikerty 

P.S. While I appreciate your offer to become the head of your harem, I find that I no longer need you. On the island there is a particularly robust species of baboon - so I went ahead and had one shaved and taught it to dance for food. It has been, thus far, a more than adequate replacement for your company.

entries.title: Letter Fragment
entries.text:
Highest Praise To You, Lord Of Theeves / Your Preesthood May Hold No Place For Me Anymores, but Afrit of Asgalun Pays wot he Ows 

It Seemz Old Mustafars Map Was True an the Isle Of Lejend Is Reel Place / May-Haps The Treshure Is Reel Too And I Shall Be The One To Find It In Yur Name 

I Know Skweeling Is A Crime That Canna Be Forgave But For That I Gave My Tung And no only the gratest Of Scores Will Settle the Balanse. 

But Ifs I Dont Return With The Treshure Kno That I Gaive My Life Freely To Settle The Scor

Praise In Yur Name, 

Afrit of Asgalun

entries.title: Letter from Prince Kraxus
entries.text:
Dear Sir,

As a Prince of the Royal Line of Stygia, I must ask that we discuss terms. I have claimed this island by might and the right of sovereignty passed down by the Kings of Stygia since the days of Acheron. 

Now I find your ships attacking my fleet at every turn in our honest attempts to bring law and order to the western straits! You and your buccaneers are causing untold damage to my fleet - and I will not stand for it! 

I demand that we meet to discuss terms at your soonest convenience. 

Sincerely, 

Prince Kraxus of Stygia

entries.title: Letter from Prince Kraxus
entries.text:
Dear Captain, 

My spies report that you barely speak a civilized tongue, let alone read. I will have my messenger read this missive aloud to you in your own barbaric tongue - probably before you slay and eat him, as I have been told you are wont to do. 

I feel that my attempts to negotiate with you have been in vain. No matter how much one tries to speak to a savage, it seems the only language you truly understand is blood and pain. You have landed an army of riff-raff on the western side of the island and are hampering my efforts to secure the shipping lanes. 

I issue this ultimatum - hopefully you understand threats. 

Attack my ships or my men again, and I will bring my full might to bear against you and your half-trained crew of fishbait. I will have you flayed and use your skin as the covering for a chair cushion - such that even in death you will be beneath me, kissing my buttocks for the rest of my life. 

Prince Kraxus of Stygia

entries.title: Letter from Prince Kraxus
entries.text:
Savage, 

It is a shame you didn't eat my last messenger, since I had pumped his veins full of a slow acting poison. More's the pity. 

However, I did enjoy his description of how my last message sent you into a frothing rage which saw you biting at the furniture and smashing the heads of your own men. Such leadership! 

Tell me, did you learn this behavior from the gorillas that raised you, or is this a side effect of the so- called demon blood that runs through your veins? Frankly, even though I was raised in Stygia and saw all kinds of ritual sacrifices, I don't have much faith in sorcery. Even the storms which whip across this island don't seem much more than a localized weather phenomenon. And you - well I doubt that you are little more than a pale-skinned mutant - a fruit that fell a bit far from the tree of humanity and started rotting on the ground. 

I'd say I look forward to meeting you on the battlefield, but let's be honest. We won't meet there. I have hundreds of soldiers to do that kind of thing. I'll tell them to keep their eyes peeled for a gibbering slug though. 

Cheerio, 

Kraxus

entries.title: Leyshrine research notes
entries.text:
These are my notes on the working of the leyshrine, translated by Siptah with his notes in the Year of the Lemur.

'This is the Leyshrine of the Fiends' * - Translation note - seems to be referring one or several of the Elder Races.

'The gods have given us a means to summon bounty*' - Translation note - could also mean sacrifice.

'Raw chaos* and … . containment*' - Translation note - fragments missing. There is a diagram of some container here.

'The Maelstrom will power the… surge*' - Translation note - multiple words could fit here. Surge is my choice.

 'The Yuggoth* stone will be… ' - Translation note - Yuggoth* is also mentioned in the Book of Skelos. Must cross-reference.

From what I can tell, each of the Leyshrines is tied to a different type of summon. Possibly a preference of the different Elder Races in resources. Experimentation will be required.
